    "output": "Chunk ID: 116056\nWall time: 0.0509 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 5411\nOutput:\n# SPEC_7.md — FR7: Offer & Coupon Engine → Eligibility Check → Discount Application\n\n**Owner:** promotions  \n**Module(s):** `promotions` (primary), `shared/eventbrite/` (`EbDiscountSyncService` ACL), `booking-inventory` (event consumer — cart total recompute), `payments-ticketing` (event source — `BookingConfirmedEvent`, `BookingCancelledEvent`), `identity` (event source — `PaymentFailedEvent` via payments-ticketing)  \n**Flow:** Flow 7 — Admin Creates Promotion → Coupon Created Internally → EB Outbound Sync → User Applies Coupon → Eligibility Check → Discount Applied to Cart → Post-Redemption Lifecycle → EB Inbound Reconciliation  \n**Last Updated:** March 5, 2026  \n**Status:** Stages 1–8 Complete — Ready for implementation\n\n> **Pre-FR7 Gap Fixes Applied (2026-03-05)**\n> Six blocking gaps discovered during flows 1–6 audit were fixed before FR7 implementation begins.\n> Full details: [`docs/Files/PRE_FR7_GAP_FIXES.md`](../../docs/Files/PRE_FR7_GAP_FIXES.md)\n>\n> | Gap | Fix Summary | Status |\n> |---|---|---|\n> | GAP-1 | `BookingConfirmedEvent` — added `bookingId` as first field; `PaymentService` updated to pass `booking.getId()` | ✅ Applied |\n> | GAP-2 | `CartSummaryDto` created in `shared/dto/`; `getCartSummary(Long cartId)` added to `CartSnapshotReader`; `CartSnapshotReaderImpl` updated | ✅ Applied |\n> | GAP-3 | `CartService.confirm()` now subtracts `cart.couponDiscountAmount` from Stripe total before publishing `CartAssembledEvent`; floor at zero guard added | ✅ Applied |\n> | GAP-4 | V36 migration renames `discount_amount` → `group_discount_amount`; adds `coupon_discount_amount` column; `Cart` entity split into `groupDiscountAmount` + `couponDiscountAmount`; `CartPricingService.recompute()` only writes `groupDiscountAmount` | ✅ Applied |\n> | GAP-5 | `promotions` module scaffolded; `promotions/pom.xml` created; added to root `pom.xml` and `app/pom.xml` | ✅ Applied |\n> | GAP-6 | `CouponAppliedEvent` and `CouponValidatedEvent` created in `shared/common/event/published/` | ✅ Applied |\n\n**Depends on:**\n- `SPEC_4.md` (FR4) — `CartAssembledEvent` carries `couponCode`; `CartSnapshotReader` available in `shared/` with `getCartItems()` **and `getCartSummary()` (added — GAP-2)**; cart `orgId` and `slotId` accessible via `getCartSummary()`\n- `SPEC_5.md` (FR5) — `BookingConfirmedEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId)` published **(updated — GAP-1: `bookingId` added as first field)**; `BookingCancelledEvent` published; `PaymentFailedEvent(cartId, userId)` published — all in `shared/`\n- `SPEC_3.MD` (FR3) — JWT with `sub=userId`, `role`, `orgId` claims required for all protected endpoints\n\n**Key Architecture Decision:** Discounts are computed and enforced **entirely internally**. The discounted amount is applied to the Stripe `PaymentIntent` amount (FR5). Eventbrite discount sync (`EbDiscountSyncService`) is a **passive marketing mirror only** — it is never on the payment critical path. Because Eventbrite has no PUT/PATCH for discounts, any modification to an EB-synced discount requires a guarded **delete + recreate** pattern. If EB has recorded `quantity_sold > 0` for the discount, deletion is blocked by EB and must be skipped gracefully.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 1 — Requirements\n\n### Area A — Promotion & Coupon Management (Admin, Internal)\n\n- `[MUST]` Admin creates a promotion with: `name`, `discountType` (`PERCENT_OFF` / `AMOUNT_OFF`), `discountValue`, `scope` (`ORG_WIDE` / `EVENT_SCOPED`), `ebEventId` (nullable — required only for `EVENT_SCOPED`), `validFrom`, `validUntil`, `maxUsageLimit`, `perUserCap`\n- `[MUST]` Admin creates a coupon code and links it to a promotion; coupon `code` must be unique within the same `orgId`\n- `[MUST]` Admin can deactivate a promotion — cascades `INACTIVE` status to all linked coupons; triggers guarded EB delete for each synced coupon\n- `[MUST]` Admin can update a promotion's `validFrom`, `validUntil`, or `maxUsageLimit` — internal update persists first; EB re-sync triggered via delete + recreate (no PUT/PATCH exists on Eventbrite)\n- `[MUST]` Admin can deactivate a single coupon — triggers guarded EB delete for that coupon\n- `[MUST]` Duplicate coupon `code` within the same `orgId` is rejected with `409 COUPON_CODE_CONFLICT`\n- `[SHOULD]` Admin can list all promotions and coupons for their org with current redemption statistics\n- `[SHOULD]` Admin can view per-coupon `ebSyncStatus` and last sync timestamp\n\n### Area B — EB Outbound Sync (Internal → Eventbrite)\n\n- `[MUST]` After internal coupon creation, asynchronously push to Eventbrite via `EbDiscountSyncService.createDiscount(orgId, payload)` → `POST /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/`\n- `[MUST]` Store the returned `eb_discount_id` on the `Coupon` record; mark `eb_sync_status = SYNCED`\n- `[MUST]` If EB create call fails, mark `eb_sync_status = SYNC_FAILED`; coupon is still `ACTIVE` and usable internally — EB sync failure is non-blocking\n- `[MUST]` After any successful EB create, verify the discount exists via `GET /discounts/{eb_discount_id}/` and reconcile key fields; on 404 set `SYNC_FAILED`\n- `[MUST]` **Modification rule (no PUT/PATCH):** to update an EB-synced discount: (1) fetch current EB `quantity_sold` via `GET /discounts/{eb_discount_id}/`; (2) if `quantity_sold == 0`: call `DELETE /discounts/{eb_discount_id}/` then `POST /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` with updated fields then store new `eb_discount_id` as `SYNCED`; (3) if `quantity_sold > 0`: set `eb_sync_status = CANNOT_RESYNC`, persist internal change only, alert ops — never attempt delete when EB has active usage\n- `[MUST]` **Guarded delete rule:** before any EB DELETE call, fetch current `quantity_sold` via `GET /discounts/{eb_discount_id}/`; if `quantity_sold > 0`, set `eb_sync_status = DELETE_BLOCKED`, log ops alert, continue without deleting; if `quantity_sold == 0`, proceed with DELETE\n- `[MUST]` **Idempotency guard on create:** before calling EB create, scan `GET /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` for an existing discount with the same `code`; if found, adopt the existing `eb_discount_id` as `SYNCED` instead of creating a duplicate\n- `[MUST]` `SYNC_FAILED` coupons are eligible for retry on next `CouponExpiryJob` scheduled run — no immediate re-throw\n- `[MUST]` If `promotion.scope = EVENT_SCOPED` and `promotion.ebEventId` is `null`, skip EB sync entirely and log a warning; coupon is still usable internally\n- `[SHOULD]` Record `last_eb_sync_at` and `eb_quantity_sold_at_last_sync` on each sync attempt\n\n### Area C — EB Inbound Sync (Eventbrite → Internal)\n\n- `[MUST]` `DiscountReconciliationJob` runs on a configurable schedule (default: every 4 hours) and calls `GET /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` to list all EB discounts per org\n- `[MUST]` For each EB discount, find the matching internal `Coupon` by `eb_discount_id`:\n  - **Match + `quantity_sold` drifted higher:** update internal `redemption_count` to EB value (EB wins on usage counts); log drift\n  - **Match + fields drifted** (code or discount value changed on EB platform): set `eb_sync_status = DRIFT_DETECTED`, alert ops; do NOT auto-overwrite internal record — internal DB is source of truth\n  - **No internal match (orphan):** create `OrphanEbDiscount` audit record; alert ops; do NOT auto-import or create internal coupon\n  - **Internal coupon is `SYNCED` but EB returns 404:** set `eb_sync_status = EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY`; alert ops; coupon remains usable internally\n- `[MUST]` If the EB list call fails for an org, log error, skip that org, write a `DiscountReconciliationLog` with error summary, and continue processing remaining orgs\n- `[MUST]` Write a `DiscountReconciliationLog` after every run with: `orgId`, `runAt`, `discountsChecked`, `driftsFound`, `orphansFound`, `externallyDeletedFound`, `actionsTakenSummary`\n- `[SHOULD]` Reconciliation job is protected by a distributed lock (ShedLock) to prevent parallel runs\n- `[WONT]` Auto-applying or auto-importing external EB discount codes to internal orders without admin approval\n\n### Area D — Coupon Eligibility & Application (User Flow)\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `POST /api/v1/promotions/validate` accepting `{ couponCode, cartId }` (JWT `ROLE_USER`)\n- `[MUST]` Eligibility checks run in this exact order:\n  1. Coupon exists and `status = ACTIVE`\n  2. `NOW()` is within `[validFrom, validUntil]`\n  3. Cart's `orgId` matches `coupon.orgId` (read via `CartSnapshotReader`)\n  4. If `scope = EVENT_SCOPED`: cart's `slotId` maps to `promotion.ebEventId`\n  5. `redemption_count + active_reservations_count < maxUsageLimit`\n  6. User's prior non-voided redemptions for this coupon `< perUserCap`\n- `[MUST]` On successful validation, create a `CouponUsageReservation(couponId, cartId, userId, expiresAt = cart.expiresAt)` — this reservation counts toward the usage limit for subsequent checks\n- `[MUST]` Return `DiscountBreakdownResponse` with: `couponCode`, `discountType`, `discountAmount`, `adjustedCartTotal`, `currency`\n- `[MUST]` Idempotency: if the same `(couponCode, cartId)` combination already has an active `CouponUsageReservation`, return the existing `DiscountBreakdownResponse` without creating a duplicate reservation\n- `[MUST]` Publish `CouponAppliedEvent(cartId, couponCode, discountAmountInSmallestUnit, currency, userId)` when `CartAssembledEvent` is received by `promotions` and the cart snapshot contains a `couponCode`\n- `[MUST]` `booking-inventory` `CouponAppliedListener` listens to `CouponAppliedEvent` and recomputes cart total with the discounted amount\n- `[SHOULD]` Expose `DELETE /api/v1/promotions/cart/{cartId}/coupon` to allow a user to remove a coupon from their cart — releases the `CouponUsageReservation`\n\n### Area E — Post-Redemption Lifecycle\n\n- `[MUST]` On `BookingConfirmedEvent(bookingId, cartId, ...)` **(note: `bookingId` is now the first field — GAP-1 fix)**: look up active `CouponUsageReservation` by `cartId`; if found, create `CouponRedemption(couponId, userId, bookingId, cartId, discountAmount, currency, redeemedAt)`; increment `redemption_count`; mark reservation `released = true`; if `redemption_count >= maxUsageLimit`, mark coupon `EXHAUSTED` and trigger guarded EB delete\n- `[MUST]` On `BookingCancelledEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, userId, reason)`: find non-voided `CouponRedemption` by `bookingId`; set `voided = true`, `voidedAt = NOW()`; decrement `redemption_count`; if coupon was `EXHAUSTED`, revert status to `ACTIVE`; do NOT attempt EB re-create if coupon had `DELETE_BLOCKED` or `EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY`\n- `[MUST]` On `PaymentFailedEvent(cartId, userId)`: find and release any open `CouponUsageReservation` for this `cartId` by setting `released = true`\n- `[SHOULD]` Expose `GET /api/v1/promotions/{couponCode}/usage` for admin to view `redemptionCount`, `activeReservations`, `voidedRedemptions`\n\n### Area F — Error & Edge Cases\n\n- `[MUST]` EB sync failure never blocks internal coupon creation or user-facing eligibility check — the two concerns are fully decoupled\n- `[MUST]` EB `DISCOUNT_CANNOT_BE_DELETED` (HTTP 400, quantity_sold > 0) handled gracefully: log, set `DELETE_BLOCKED`, continue; no exception propagated to caller\n- `[MUST]` If internal and EB records exist but `eb_discount_id` is stale or mismatched (drift), set `DRIFT_DETECTED` for manual reconciliation — never auto-fix silently\n- `[MUST]` If `eb_event_id` is `null` on event-scoped promotion, skip EB sync and log warning; do not throw\n- `[SHOULD]` Before any EB create, run idempotency guard: check `GET /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` for matching `code`; adopt if found — prevents phantom duplicates on retry\n- `[WONT]` Per-seat, per-ticket-class, or wallet-credit-specific coupon scoping in FR7\n- `[WONT]` Email notification on coupon exhaustion or expiry in FR7\n\n---\n\n## Stage 2 — Domain Model\n\nA `Promotion` defines the discount rule: type, value, validity window, scope, and usage limits. A `Coupon` is a redeemable code linked to one `Promotion`; it carries its own lifecycle status and all state related to Eventbrite sync (`eb_discount_id`, `eb_sync_status`, `eb_quantity_sold_at_last_sync`). A `CouponRedemption` is an immutable record created exactly once per confirmed booking that used a coupon — it is soft-voided on cancellation, never deleted. A `CouponUsageReservation` is a soft-hold created when a user successfully validates a coupon during cart session; it is released on payment failure, cart expiry, or coupon removal. `OrphanEbDiscount` and `DiscountReconciliationLog` are operational audit records owned exclusively by the `promotions` module.\n\n### Entities\n\n#### `Promotion`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `orgId`, `name`, `discountType` (`PERCENT_OFF` / `AMOUNT_OFF`), `discountValue` (BigDecimal), `scope` (`ORG_WIDE` / `EVENT_SCOPED`), `ebEventId` (String, nullable — null for `ORG_WIDE`), `maxUsageLimit` (Integer, nullable — null = unlimited), `perUserCap` (Integer, nullable — null = unlimited), `validFrom` (OffsetDateTime), `validUntil` (OffsetDateTime), `status` (`ACTIVE` / `INACTIVE`), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`\n- **Lifecycle:** `ACTIVE → INACTIVE` (admin deactivate or all linked coupons exhausted)\n- **Domain behaviour:**\n  - `deactivate()` — sets `status = INACTIVE`, cascades `deactivate()` to all linked `Coupon` records\n  - `isValid(Instant now)` — returns true when `status = ACTIVE` and `now` is within `[validFrom, validUntil]`\n- **Relationships:** 1 `Promotion` → N `Coupon`\n\n#### `Coupon`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `promotionId` (FK → promotions), `orgId`, `code` (String), `status` (`ACTIVE` / `INACTIVE` / `EXHAUSTED`), `redemptionCount` (int, default 0), `ebDiscountId` (String, nullable), `ebSyncStatus` (`NOT_SYNCED` / `SYNC_PENDING` / `SYNCED` / `SYNC_FAILED` / `DELETE_BLOCKED` / `EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY` / `DRIFT_DETECTED` / `CANNOT_RESYNC`), `ebQuantitySoldAtLastSync` (Integer, nullable), `lastEbSyncAt` (OffsetDateTime, nullable), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`\n- **Lifecycle:** `ACTIVE → INACTIVE` (admin/cascade) | `ACTIVE → EXHAUSTED` (redemptionCount >= maxUsageLimit) | `EXHAUSTED → ACTIVE` (redemption voided)\n- **Domain behaviour:**\n  - `recordRedemption(int maxUsageLimit)` — increments `redemptionCount`; if `redemptionCount >= maxUsageLimit`, sets `status = EXHAUSTED`\n  - `voidRedemption()` — decrements `redemptionCount`; if `status = EXHAUSTED`, reverts to `ACTIVE`\n  - `markSynced(String ebDiscountId)` — sets `ebDiscountId`, `ebSyncStatus = SYNCED`, `lastEbSyncAt = NOW()`\n  - `markSyncFailed()` — sets `ebSyncStatus = SYNC_FAILED`\n  - `canEbDelete()` — returns true when `ebDiscountId != null && ebQuantitySoldAtLastSync == 0`\n- **Relationships:** N `Coupon` → 1 `Promotion`; 1 `Coupon` → N `CouponRedemption`; 1 `Coupon` → N `CouponUsageReservation`\n\n#### `CouponRedemption`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `couponId` (FK → coupons), `userId` (UUID, ID-only reference to `identity`), `bookingId` (UUID, ID-only reference to `payments-ticketing`), `cartId` (UUID, ID-only reference to `booking-inventory`), `discountAmount` (BigDecimal), `currency` (String), `redeemedAt` (OffsetDateTime), `voided` (boolean, default false), `voidedAt` (OffsetDateTime, nullable)\n- **Lifecycle:** created once on `BookingConfirmedEvent`; `voided = true` on `BookingCancelledEvent` — never physically deleted\n- **Constraints:** at most one non-voided `CouponRedemption` per `(couponId, bookingId)`\n\n#### `CouponUsageReservation`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `couponId` (FK → coupons), `cartId` (UUID), `userId` (UUID), `reservedAt` (OffsetDateTime), `expiresAt` (OffsetDateTime — matches `cart.expiresAt`), `released` (boolean, default false)\n- **Lifecycle:** created on successful coupon validation; released on `PaymentFailedEvent`, coupon removal, cart expiry, or conversion to `CouponRedemption` on booking confirmation\n- **Constraints:** at most one active (non-released, non-expired) reservation per `(couponId, cartId)`\n\n#### `OrphanEbDiscount`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `ebDiscountId` (String), `orgId`, `code` (String), `detectedAt` (OffsetDateTime), `reviewed` (boolean, default false), `notes` (String, nullable)\n- **Purpose:** audit-only record for EB discounts that have no matching internal coupon; never auto-imported\n\n#### `DiscountReconciliationLog`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `orgId`, `runAt` (OffsetDateTime), `discountsChecked` (int), `driftsFound` (int), `orphansFound` (int), `externallyDeletedFound` (int), `actionsTakenSummary` (String), `errorSummary` (String, nullable)\n- **Purpose:** operational audit trail; one record per reconciliation job run per org\n\n### Enums\n\n| Enum | Values |\n|---|---|\n| `DiscountType` | `PERCENT_OFF`, `AMOUNT_OFF` |\n| `PromotionScope` | `ORG_WIDE`, `EVENT_SCOPED` |\n| `CouponStatus` | `ACTIVE`, `INACTIVE`, `EXHAUSTED` |\n| `EbSyncStatus` | `NOT_SYNCED`, `SYNC_PENDING`, `SYNCED`, `SYNC_FAILED`, `DELETE_BLOCKED`, `EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY`, `DRIFT_DETECTED`, `CANNOT_RESYNC` |\n\n### Non-Persisted Value Objects\n\n#### `DiscountCalculationResult`\n```java\npublic record DiscountCalculationResult(\n    String couponCode,\n    DiscountType discountType,\n    long discountAmountInSmallestUnit,   // e.g. paise/cents\n    long adjustedTotalInSmallestUnit,\n    String currency\n) {}\n```\n- Computed by `CouponEligibilityService.calculateDiscount()`; returned to controller and published in events; **never persisted**\n\n#### `EbDiscountSyncPayload`\n```java\npublic record EbDiscountSyncPayload(\n    String code,\n    String type,             // \"percent\" or \"amount\"\n    Double percentOff,       // nullable\n    Double amountOff,        // nullable, in major currency unit\n    String startDate,        // ISO-8601\n    String endDate,          // ISO-8601\n    Integer quantityAvailable,\n    String eventId,          // nullable\n    List<String> ticketClassIds  // nullable\n) {}\n```\n- Mapped from `Coupon` + `Promotion` by `EbDiscountSyncPayloadMapper` before any EB API call; **never persisted**\n\n### Cross-Module ID References (no entity imports)\n\n| Field | Points To | Owning Module |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Coupon.orgId` | organization context | identity / scheduling |\n| `Promotion.ebEventId` | `show_slots.eb_event_id` | scheduling (nullable) |\n| `CouponRedemption.userId` | `users.id` | identity |\n| `CouponRedemption.bookingId` | `bookings.id` | payments-ticketing |\n| `CouponRedemption.cartId` | `carts.id` | booking-inventory |\n| `CouponUsageReservation.cartId` | `carts.id` | booking-inventory |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 3 — Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Architecture Pattern\nModular Monolith — `promotions` is the sole domain owner. Stripe and EB are never called directly from any module; only `shared/` ACL facades are used. Cross-module data reads use the existing `CartSnapshotReader` shared interface.\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Layer | Owner |\n|---|---|\n| Domain entities + business rules | `promotions` |\n| EB sync calls | `EbDiscountSyncService` in `shared/eventbrite/service/` |\n| Cart org/slot read on validate | `CartSnapshotReader` in `shared/common/service/` (implemented by `booking-inventory`) |\n| Cart total recompute on discount | `booking-inventory` (`CouponAppliedListener`) |\n| Redemption trigger | `promotions` listens to `BookingConfirmedEvent` from `shared/` |\n| Redemption void | `promotions` listens to `BookingCancelledEvent` from `shared/` |\n| Reservation release | `promotions` listens to `PaymentFailedEvent` from `shared/` |\n\n### Inter-Module Communication\n\n| Interaction | Direction | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Read cart `orgId` + `slotId` + `couponCode` on validate | `promotions` → `booking-inventory` | `CartSnapshotReader.getCartSummary()` shared interface — **`getCartSummary()` added (GAP-2)** |\n| Discount applied to cart total | `promotions` → `booking-inventory` | `CouponAppliedEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent |\n| Record redemption + increment count | `payments-ticketing` → `promotions` | `BookingConfirmedEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent **(now carries `bookingId` — GAP-1)** |\n| Void redemption + decrement count | `payments-ticketing` → `promotions` | `BookingCancelledEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent |\n| Release reservation on failure | `payments-ticketing` → `promotions` | `PaymentFailedEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent |\n| EB discount create/delete | `promotions` → EB | `EbDiscountSyncService` in `shared/eventbrite/service/` |\n| EB discount list/get for reconciliation | `promotions` → EB | `EbDiscountSyncService` in `shared/eventbrite/service/` |\n\n### Shared additions\n\n> **Status: All shared additions below were created as part of the pre-FR7 gap fixes (GAP-2, GAP-6). These files already exist on disk.**\n\n```\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/\n├── CouponAppliedEvent.java          ✅ Created (GAP-6 pre-FR7 fix)\n└── CouponValidatedEvent.java        ✅ Created (GAP-6 pre-FR7 fix)\n\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/\n└── CartSummaryDto.java              ✅ Created (GAP-2 pre-FR7 fix)\n\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/\n└── CartSnapshotReader.java          ✅ getCartSummary(Long cartId) method added (GAP-2 pre-FR7 fix)\n\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/\n└── BookingConfirmedEvent.java       ✅ bookingId added as first field (GAP-1 pre-FR7 fix)\n```\n\n**`CouponAppliedEvent`** (record — already exists):\n```java\npublic record CouponAppliedEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    String couponCode,\n    long discountAmountInSmallestUnit,\n"
  }
}
```

The discounted amount is applied to the Stripe `PaymentIntent` amount (FR5). Eventbrite discount sync (`EbDiscountSyncService`) is a **passive marketing mirror only** — it is never on the payment critical path. Because Eventbrite has no PUT/PATCH for discounts, any modification to an EB-synced discount requires a guarded **delete + recreate** pattern. If EB has recorded `quantity_sold > 0` for the discount, deletion is blocked by EB and must be skipped gracefully.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 1 — Requirements\n\n### Area A — Promotion & Coupon Management (Admin, Internal)\n\n- `[MUST]` Admin creates a promotion with: `name`, `discountType` (`PERCENT_OFF` / `AMOUNT_OFF`), `discountValue`, `scope` (`ORG_WIDE` / `EVENT_SCOPED`), `ebEventId` (nullable — required only for `EVENT_SCOPED`), `validFrom`, `validUntil`, `maxUsageLimit`, `perUserCap`\n- `[MUST]` Admin creates a coupon code and links it to a promotion; (3) if `quantity_sold > 0`: set `eb_sync_status = CANNOT_RESYNC`, persist internal change only, alert ops — never attempt delete when EB has active usage\n- `[MUST]` **Guarded delete rule:** before any EB DELETE call, fetch current `quantity_sold` via `GET /discounts/{eb_discount_id}/`; coupon is still usable internally\n- `[SHOULD]` Record `last_eb_sync_at` and `eb_quantity_sold_at_last_sync` on each sync attempt\n\n### Area C — EB Inbound Sync (Eventbrite → Internal)\n\n- `[MUST]` `DiscountReconciliationJob` runs on a configurable schedule (default: every 4 hours) and calls `GET /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` to list all EB discounts per org\n- `[MUST]` For each EB discount, find the matching internal `Coupon` by `eb_discount_id`:\n  - **Match + `quantity_sold` drifted higher:** update internal `redemption_count` to EB value (EB wins on usage counts); log drift\n  - **Match + fields drifted** (code or discount value changed on EB platform): set `eb_sync_status = DRIFT_DETECTED`, alert ops; do NOT auto-overwrite internal record — internal DB is source of truth\n  - **No internal match (orphan):** create `OrphanEbDiscount` audit record; alert ops; do NOT auto-import or create internal coupon\n  - **Internal coupon is `SYNCED` but EB returns 404:** set `eb_sync_status = EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY`; alert ops; coupon remains usable internally\n- `[MUST]` If the EB list call fails for an org, log error, skip that org, write a `DiscountReconciliationLog` with error summary, and continue processing remaining orgs\n- `[MUST]` Write a `DiscountReconciliationLog` after every run with: `orgId`, `runAt`, `discountsChecked`, `driftsFound`, `orphansFound`, `externallyDeletedFound`, `actionsTakenSummary`\n- `[SHOULD]` Reconciliation job is protected by a distributed lock (ShedLock) to prevent parallel runs\n- `[WONT]` Auto-applying or auto-importing external EB discount codes to internal orders without admin approval\n\n### Area D — Coupon Eligibility & Application (User Flow)\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `POST /api/v1/promotions/validate` accepting `{ couponCode, cartId }` (JWT `ROLE_USER`)\n- `[MUST]` Eligibility checks run in this exact order:\n  1. Coupon exists and `status = ACTIVE`\n  2. `NOW()` is within `[validFrom, validUntil]`\n  3. Cart's `orgId` matches `coupon.orgId` (read via `CartSnapshotReader`)\n  4. If `scope = EVENT_SCOPED`: cart's `slotId` maps to `promotion.ebEventId`\n  5. `redemption_count + active_reservations_count < maxUsageLimit`\n  6. User's prior non-voided redemptions for this coupon `< perUserCap`\n- `[MUST]` On successful validation, create a `CouponUsageReservation(couponId, cartId, userId, expiresAt = cart.expiresAt)` — this reservation counts toward the usage limit for subsequent checks\n- `[MUST]` Return `DiscountBreakdownResponse` with: `couponCode`, `discountType`, `discountAmount`, `adjustedCartTotal`, `currency`\n- `[MUST]` Idempotency: if the same `(couponCode, cartId)` combination already has an active `CouponUsageReservation`, return the existing `DiscountBreakdownResponse` without creating a duplicate reservation\n- `[MUST]` Publish `CouponAppliedEvent(cartId, couponCode, discountAmountInSmallestUnit, currency, userId)` when `CartAssembledEvent` is received by `promotions` and the cart snapshot contains a `couponCode`\n- `[MUST]` `booking-inventory` `CouponAppliedListener` listens to `CouponAppliedEvent` and recomputes cart total with the discounted amount\n- `[SHOULD]` Expose `DELETE /api/v1/promotions/cart/{cartId}/coupon` to allow a user to remove a coupon from their cart — releases the `CouponUsageReservation`\n\n### Area E — Post-Redemption Lifecycle\n\n- `[MUST]` On `BookingConfirmedEvent(bookingId, cartId, ...)` **(note: `bookingId` is now the first field — GAP-1 fix)**: look up active `CouponUsageReservation` by `cartId`; if found, create `CouponRedemption(couponId, userId, bookingId, cartId, discountAmount, currency, redeemedAt)`; increment `redemption_count`; mark reservation `released = true`; if `redemption_count >= maxUsageLimit`, mark coupon `EXHAUSTED` and trigger guarded EB delete\n- `[MUST]` On `BookingCancelledEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, userId, reason)`: find non-voided `CouponRedemption` by `bookingId`; set `voided = true`, `voidedAt = NOW()`; decrement `redemption_count`; if coupon was `EXHAUSTED`, revert status to `ACTIVE`; do NOT attempt EB re-create if coupon had `DELETE_BLOCKED` or `EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY`\n- `[MUST]` On `PaymentFailedEvent(cartId, userId)`: find and release any open `CouponUsageReservation` for this `cartId` by setting `released = true`\n- `[SHOULD]` Expose `GET /api/v1/promotions/{couponCode}/usage` for admin to view `redemptionCount`, `activeReservations`, `voidedRedemptions`\n\n### Area F — Error & Edge Cases\n\n- `[MUST]` EB sync failure never blocks internal coupon creation or user-facing eligibility check — the two concerns are fully decoupled\n- `[MUST]` EB `DISCOUNT_CANNOT_BE_DELETED` (HTTP 400, quantity_sold > 0) handled gracefully: log, set `DELETE_BLOCKED`, continue; no exception propagated to caller\n- `[MUST]` If internal and EB records exist but `eb_discount_id` is stale or mismatched (drift), set `DRIFT_DETECTED` for manual reconciliation — never auto-fix silently\n- `[MUST]` If `eb_event_id` is `null` on event-scoped promotion, skip EB sync and log warning; do not throw\n- `[SHOULD]` Before any EB create, run idempotency guard: check `GET /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` for matching `code`; adopt if found — prevents phantom duplicates on retry\n- `[WONT]` Per-seat, per-ticket-class, or wallet-credit-specific coupon scoping in FR7\n- `[WONT]` Email notification on coupon exhaustion or expiry in FR7\n\n---\n\n## Stage 2 — Domain Model\n\nA `Promotion` defines the discount rule: type, value, validity window, scope, and usage limits. A `Coupon` is a redeemable code linked to one `Promotion`; it carries its own lifecycle status and all state related to Eventbrite sync (`eb_discount_id`, `eb_sync_status`, `eb_quantity_sold_at_last_sync`). A `CouponRedemption` is an immutable record created exactly once per confirmed booking that used a coupon — it is soft-voided on cancellation, never deleted. A `CouponUsageReservation` is a soft-hold created when a user successfully validates a coupon during cart session; it is released on payment failure, cart expiry, or coupon removal. `OrphanEbDiscount` and `DiscountReconciliationLog` are operational audit records owned exclusively by the `promotions` module.\n\n### Entities\n\n#### `Promotion`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `orgId`, `name`, `discountType` (`PERCENT_OFF` / `AMOUNT_OFF`), `discountValue` (BigDecimal), `scope` (`ORG_WIDE` / `EVENT_SCOPED`), `ebEventId` (String, nullable — null for `ORG_WIDE`), `maxUsageLimit` (Integer, nullable — null = unlimited), `perUserCap` (Integer, nullable — null = unlimited), `validFrom` (OffsetDateTime), `validUntil` (OffsetDateTime), `status` (`ACTIVE` / `INACTIVE`), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`\n- **Lifecycle:** `ACTIVE → INACTIVE` (admin deactivate or all linked coupons exhausted)\n- **Domain behaviour:**\n  - `deactivate()` — sets `status = INACTIVE`, cascades `deactivate()` to all linked `Coupon` records\n  - `isValid(Instant now)` — returns true when `status = ACTIVE` and `now` is within `[validFrom, validUntil]`\n- **Relationships:** 1 `Promotion` → N `Coupon`\n\n#### `Coupon`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `promotionId` (FK → promotions), `orgId`, `code` (String), `status` (`ACTIVE` / `INACTIVE` / `EXHAUSTED`), `redemptionCount` (int, default 0), `ebDiscountId` (String, nullable), `ebSyncStatus` (`NOT_SYNCED` / `SYNC_PENDING` / `SYNCED` / `SYNC_FAILED` / `DELETE_BLOCKED` / `EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY` / `DRIFT_DETECTED` / `CANNOT_RESYNC`), `ebQuantitySoldAtLastSync` (Integer, nullable), `lastEbSyncAt` (OffsetDateTime, nullable), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`\n- **Lifecycle:** `ACTIVE → INACTIVE` (admin/cascade) | `ACTIVE → EXHAUSTED` (redemptionCount >= maxUsageLimit) | `EXHAUSTED → ACTIVE` (redemption voided)\n- **Domain behaviour:**\n  - `recordRedemption(int maxUsageLimit)` — increments `redemptionCount`; if `redemptionCount >= maxUsageLimit`, sets `status = EXHAUSTED`\n  - `voidRedemption()` — decrements `redemptionCount`; if `status = EXHAUSTED`, reverts to `ACTIVE`\n  - `markSynced(String ebDiscountId)` — sets `ebDiscountId`, `ebSyncStatus = SYNCED`, `lastEbSyncAt = NOW()`\n  - `markSyncFailed()` — sets `ebSyncStatus = SYNC_FAILED`\n  - `canEbDelete()` — returns true when `ebDiscountId != null && ebQuantitySoldAtLastSync == 0`\n- **Relationships:** N `Coupon` → 1 `Promotion`; 1 `Coupon` → N `CouponRedemption`; 1 `Coupon` → N `CouponUsageReservation`\n\n#### `CouponRedemption`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `couponId` (FK → coupons), `userId` (UUID, ID-only reference to `identity`), `bookingId` (UUID, ID-only reference to `payments-ticketing`), `cartId` (UUID, ID-only reference to `booking-inventory`), `discountAmount` (BigDecimal), `currency` (String), `redeemedAt` (OffsetDateTime), `voided` (boolean, default false), `voidedAt` (OffsetDateTime, nullable)\n- **Lifecycle:** created once on `BookingConfirmedEvent`; `voided = true` on `BookingCancelledEvent` — never physically deleted\n- **Constraints:** at most one non-voided `CouponRedemption` per `(couponId, bookingId)`\n\n#### `CouponUsageReservation`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `couponId` (FK → coupons), `cartId` (UUID), `userId` (UUID), `reservedAt` (OffsetDateTime), `expiresAt` (OffsetDateTime — matches `cart.expiresAt`), `released` (boolean, default false)\n- **Lifecycle:** created on successful coupon validation; released on `PaymentFailedEvent`, coupon removal, cart expiry, or conversion to `CouponRedemption` on booking confirmation\n- **Constraints:** at most one active (non-released, non-expired) reservation per `(couponId, cartId)`\n\n#### `OrphanEbDiscount`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `ebDiscountId` (String), `orgId`, `code` (String), `detectedAt` (OffsetDateTime), `reviewed` (boolean, default false), `notes` (String, nullable)\n- **Purpose:** audit-only record for EB discounts that have no matching internal coupon; never auto-imported\n\n#### `DiscountReconciliationLog`\n- **Identity:** `id` (UUID PK)\n- **Fields:** `orgId`, `runAt` (OffsetDateTime), `discountsChecked` (int), `driftsFound` (int), `orphansFound` (int), `externallyDeletedFound` (int), `actionsTakenSummary` (String), `errorSummary` (String, nullable)\n- **Purpose:** operational audit trail; one record per reconciliation job run per org\n\n### Enums\n\n| Enum | Values |\n|---|---|\n| `DiscountType` | `PERCENT_OFF`, `AMOUNT_OFF` |\n| `PromotionScope` | `ORG_WIDE`, `EVENT_SCOPED` |\n| `CouponStatus` | `ACTIVE`, `INACTIVE`, `EXHAUSTED` |\n| `EbSyncStatus` | `NOT_SYNCED`, `SYNC_PENDING`, `SYNCED`, `SYNC_FAILED`, `DELETE_BLOCKED`, `EB_DELETED_EXTERNALLY`, `DRIFT_DETECTED`, `CANNOT_RESYNC` |\n\n### Non-Persisted Value Objects\n\n#### `DiscountCalculationResult`\n```java\npublic record DiscountCalculationResult(\n    String couponCode,\n    DiscountType discountType,\n    long discountAmountInSmallestUnit,   // e.g. paise/cents\n    long adjustedTotalInSmallestUnit,\n    String currency\n) {}\n```\n- Computed by `CouponEligibilityService.calculateDiscount()`; returned to controller and published in events; **never persisted**\n\n#### `EbDiscountSyncPayload`\n```java\npublic record EbDiscountSyncPayload(\n    String code,\n    String type,             // \"percent\" or \"amount\"\n    Double percentOff,       // nullable\n    Double amountOff,        // nullable, in major currency unit\n    String startDate,        // ISO-8601\n    String endDate,          // ISO-8601\n    Integer quantityAvailable,\n    String eventId,          // nullable\n    List<String> ticketClassIds  // nullable\n) {}\n```\n- Mapped from `Coupon` + `Promotion` by `EbDiscountSyncPayloadMapper` before any EB API call; **never persisted**\n\n### Cross-Module ID References (no entity imports)\n\n| Field | Points To | Owning Module |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Coupon.orgId` | organization context | identity / scheduling |\n| `Promotion.ebEventId` | `show_slots.eb_event_id` | scheduling (nullable) |\n| `CouponRedemption.userId` | `users.id` | identity |\n| `CouponRedemption.bookingId` | `bookings.id` | payments-ticketing |\n| `CouponRedemption.cartId` | `carts.id` | booking-inventory |\n| `CouponUsageReservation.cartId` | `carts.id` | booking-inventory |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 3 — Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Architecture Pattern\nModular Monolith — `promotions` is the sole domain owner. Stripe and EB are never called directly from any module; only `shared/` ACL facades are used. Cross-module data reads use the existing `CartSnapshotReader` shared interface.\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Layer | Owner |\n|---|---|\n| Domain entities + business rules | `promotions` |\n| EB sync calls | `EbDiscountSyncService` in `shared/eventbrite/service/` |\n| Cart org/slot read on validate | `CartSnapshotReader` in `shared/common/service/` (implemented by `booking-inventory`) |\n| Cart total recompute on discount | `booking-inventory` (`CouponAppliedListener`) |\n| Redemption trigger | `promotions` listens to `BookingConfirmedEvent` from `shared/` |\n| Redemption void | `promotions` listens to `BookingCancelledEvent` from `shared/` |\n| Reservation release | `promotions` listens to `PaymentFailedEvent` from `shared/` |\n\n### Inter-Module Communication\n\n| Interaction | Direction | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Read cart `orgId` + `slotId` + `couponCode` on validate | `promotions` → `booking-inventory` | `CartSnapshotReader.getCartSummary()` shared interface — **`getCartSummary()` added (GAP-2)** |\n| Discount applied to cart total | `promotions` → `booking-inventory` | `CouponAppliedEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent |\n| Record redemption + increment count | `payments-ticketing` → `promotions` | `BookingConfirmedEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent **(now carries `bookingId` — GAP-1)** |\n| Void redemption + decrement count | `payments-ticketing` → `promotions` | `BookingCancelledEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent |\n| Release reservation on failure | `payments-ticketing` → `promotions` | `PaymentFailedEvent` Spring ApplicationEvent |\n| EB discount create/delete | `promotions` → EB | `EbDiscountSyncService` in `shared/eventbrite/service/` |\n| EB discount list/get for reconciliation | `promotions` → EB | `EbDiscountSyncService` in `shared/eventbrite/service/` |\n\n### Shared additions\n\n> **Status: All shared additions below were created as part of the pre-FR7 gap fixes (GAP-2, GAP-6). These files already exist on disk.**\n\n```\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/\n├── CouponAppliedEvent.java          ✅ Created (GAP-6 pre-FR7 fix)\n└── CouponValidatedEvent.java        ✅ Created (GAP-6 pre-FR7 fix)\n\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/\n└── CartSummaryDto.java              ✅ Created (GAP-2 pre-FR7 fix)\n\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/\n└── CartSnapshotReader.java          ✅ getCartSummary(Long cartId) method added (GAP-2 pre-FR7 fix)\n\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/\n└── BookingConfirmedEvent.java       ✅ bookingId added as first field (GAP-1 pre-FR7 fix)\n```\n\n**`CouponAppliedEvent`** (record — already exists):\n```java\npublic record CouponAppliedEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    String couponCode,\n    long discountAmountInSmallestUnit,\n"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T21:35:41.657Z",

    "output": "Chunk ID: 15d3ca\nWall time: 0.0522 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2322\nOutput:\n# Pre-FR7 Gap Fixes: Flows 1–6 Audit Report\n\n**Date:** 2026-03-05  \n**Scope:** Gaps in flows 1–6 that would block FR7 (Coupon & Discount Engine) implementation  \n**Status:** All 6 gaps fixed and verified (booking-inventory 80/80 ✅, payments-ticketing 67/67 ✅)\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Background](#background)\n2. [GAP-1 — `BookingConfirmedEvent` missing `bookingId`](#gap-1--bookingconfirmedevent-missing-bookingid)\n3. [GAP-2 — `CartSnapshotReader` exposes only line items, not cart header](#gap-2--cartsnapshotreader-exposes-only-line-items-not-cart-header)\n4. [GAP-3 — `CartService.confirm()` ignores coupon discount in Stripe amount](#gap-3--cartserviceconfirm-ignores-coupon-discount-in-stripe-amount)\n5. [GAP-4 — `CartPricingService.recompute()` overwrites coupon discount](#gap-4--cartpricingservicerecompute-overwrites-coupon-discount)\n6. [GAP-5 — `promotions` module not scaffolded](#gap-5--promotions-module-not-scaffolded)\n7. [GAP-6 — `CouponAppliedEvent` and `CouponValidatedEvent` missing from `shared/`](#gap-6--couponappliedevent-and-couponvalidatedevent-missing-from-shared)\n8. [Test Results After Fixes](#test-results-after-fixes)\n9. [Files Changed Index](#files-changed-index)\n\n---\n\n## Background\n\nBefore implementing FR7 (Promotions / Coupon Engine) a full code audit was performed\nacross flows 1–6.  Six gaps were found that would cause either:\n\n- **Data integrity failures** (wrong Stripe amount charged to users)\n- **Broken cross-module communication** (promotions cannot record redemptions without `bookingId`)\n- **Silent data clobbering** (coupon discount overwritten by group discount on every recompute)\n- **Compilation failures** (promotions module doesn't exist, shared events missing)\n\nAll 6 gaps were fixed in the **correct dependency order** so that no fix breaks a previous one:\n\n```\nGAP-1  →  GAP-6  →  GAP-2  →  GAP-4  →  GAP-3  →  GAP-5\nshared     shared     shared     Cart       CartSvc    scaffold\nevents     events     interface  entity +   total      promotions\n                                 migration  compute    module\n```\n\n---\n\n## GAP-1 — `BookingConfirmedEvent` missing `bookingId`\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL\n\n### Root Cause\n\n`BookingConfirmedEvent` was defined with four fields:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE (broken)\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    String stripePaymentIntentId,\n    Long userId\n) {}\n```\n\n`PaymentService.confirmPayment()` published this event without including `booking.getId()`.\n\n### Why It Blocks FR7\n\nThe `promotions` module must create a `CouponRedemption` row when a booking is confirmed.\n`CouponRedemption` has a `booking_id` foreign key so that:\n\n- The redemption is tied to a specific booking (not just a cart, which is transient)\n- Cancellations can look up the redemption via `bookingId` to trigger refund of the coupon\n- The uniqueness constraint `(coupon_id, booking_id)` prevents double-redemption\n\nWithout `bookingId` in the event, the `BookingConfirmedListener` in `promotions` would\nhave to do an extra JOIN back to `bookings` (cross-module DB coupling — **HARD RULE #1 violation**)\nor simply not be able to create the `CouponRedemption` at all.\n\n### Fix\n\n**`shared/…/event/published/BookingConfirmedEvent.java`**\n```java\n// AFTER (fixed)\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    Long bookingId,          // ← ADDED — needed by promotions.BookingConfirmedListener\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    String stripePaymentIntentId,\n    Long userId\n) {}\n```\n\n**`payments-ticketing/…/service/PaymentService.java`** — publish site updated:\n```java\n// BEFORE\nafterCommitEventPublisher.publish(new BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    booking.getCartId(),\n    seatIds,\n    paymentIntentId,\n    booking.getUserId()\n));\n\n// AFTER\nafterCommitEventPublisher.publish(new BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    booking.getId(),         // ← bookingId added\n    booking.getCartId(),\n    seatIds,\n    paymentIntentId,\n    booking.getUserId()\n));\n```\n\n**`BookingConfirmedListener` in `booking-inventory`** — unaffected (it only reads\n`cartId`, `seatIds`, `stripePaymentIntentId`, `userId` — all still present, order didn't\nchange for those accessors because Java records use named accessor methods not positional).\n\n**Tests** — `PaymentServiceTest` uses `ArgumentCaptor`, not a constructor call, so no\nchanges needed.\n\n---\n\n## GAP-2 — `CartSnapshotReader` exposes only line items, not cart header\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL\n\n### Root Cause\n\nThe `CartSnapshotReader` interface in `shared/` had only one method:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE (incomplete)\npublic interface CartSnapshotReader {\n    List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);\n}\n```\n\n`CartItemSnapshotDto` records per-line-item data (seatId, ticketClassId, unitPrice, quantity)\nbut nothing about the cart itself.\n\n### Why It Blocks FR7\n\n`CouponEligibilityService` (in `promotions`) must validate a coupon against:\n\n| Context field needed | Where it lives           |\n|----------------------|--------------------------|\n| `orgId`              | `Cart.orgId`             |\n| `slotId`             | `Cart.showSlotId`        |\n| `couponCode`         | `Cart.couponCode`        |\n| `expiresAt`          | `Cart.expiresAt`         |\n| `currency`           | `Cart.currency` (via embedded Money) |\n\nWithout this data, the eligibility check would need to:\n1. Call another module's `@Repository` directly — **HARD RULE #1 violation**, or\n2. Make a REST call to an endpoint that didn't exist, or\n3. Duplicate the query logic inside `promotions` — wrong\n\n### Fix\n\n**New shared DTO** — `shared/…/dto/CartSummaryDto.java`:\n```java\npublic record CartSummaryDto(\n    Long    cartId,\n    Long    orgId,\n    Long    slotId,\n    String  couponCode,\n    Instant expiresAt,\n    String  currency\n) {}\n```\n\n**Extended interface** — `CartSnapshotReader.java`:\n```java\npublic interface CartSnapshotReader {\n    List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);\n\n    /**\n     * Returns cart-level header metadata.\n     * Used by promotions.CouponEligibilityService.\n     * Throws ResourceNotFoundException if cart does not exist.\n     */\n    CartSummaryDto getCartSummary(Long cartId);\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation** — `CartSnapshotReaderImpl.java` in `booking-inventory`:\n- Added `CartRepository` dependency via constructor injection\n- New `getCartSummary()` method loads the cart by ID and maps its fields to `CartSummaryDto`\n- Throws `ResourceNotFoundException(404)` if cart not found — consistent error contract\n\n---\n\n## GAP-3 — `CartService.confirm()` ignores coupon discount in Stripe amount\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL (financial bug — user over-charged)\n\n### Root Cause\n\n`CartService.confirm()` computed the Stripe PaymentIntent amount as:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE (financially incorrect)\nCartPricingResult pricing = cartPricingService.recompute(cart, confirmedItems);\nlong totalAmountInSmallestUnit = pricing.total().amount()\n    .multiply(java.math.BigDecimal.valueOf(100)).longValue();\n```\n\n`CartPricingResult.total()` only accounts for the group/bulk discount\n(`subtotal − groupDiscount`).  The coupon discount stored on `cart.couponDiscountAmount`\nwas never read here.\n\n### Impact if Unfixed\n\n1. User applies a ₹500 coupon to a ₹2000 cart\n2. Promotions module writes `couponDiscountAmount = 500.00` to the cart\n3. Cart confirm is triggered — the amount Stripe receives is **₹2000** (net of only the\n   group discount, ignoring the coupon)\n4. User is billed the **full pre-coupon price**\n5. The coupon usage counter increments, but no actual discount was given\n\n### Fix\n\n```java\n// AFTER (fixed)\nCartPricingResult pricing = cartPricingService.recompute(cart, confirmedItems);\n\n// Subtract coupon discount (written by promotions via CouponAppliedEvent) from the\n// post-group-discount total.  Floor at zero to guard against oversized coupons.\njava.math.BigDecimal netTotal = pricing.total().amount()\n    .subtract(cart.getCouponDiscountAmount().amount())\n    .max(java.math.BigDecimal.ZERO);                         // ← safety floor\nlong totalAmountInSmallestUnit = netTotal\n    .multiply(java.math.BigDecimal.valueOf(100))\n    .setScale(0, java.math.RoundingMode.HALF_UP)\n    .longValue();\nString pricingCurrency = pricing.total().currency().toLowerCase();\n```\n\nThe `.max(BigDecimal.ZERO)` guard ensures that even if an edge-case coupon value exceeds\nthe remaining total (e.g., a 100%-off promotional code), the Stripe amount is clamped at\nzero rather than going negative (which would cause a Stripe API error).\n\n**Dependency:** This fix depends on GAP-4 (the `getCouponDiscountAmount()` method must\nexist on `Cart`) — hence GAP-4 was applied first.\n\n---\n\n## GAP-4 — `CartPricingService.recompute()` overwrites coupon discount\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL (data clobbering — discount silently lost)\n\n### Root Cause\n\n`Cart.java` had a **single `discountAmount` field** mapped to a single `discount_amount`\ndatabase column.  `CartPricingService.recompute()` always wrote the group discount into it:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE — overwrites whatever was there\ncart.setDiscountAmount(discount);  // discount = group discount only\n```\n\nIf the promotions module wrote a coupon discount to the cart (e.g., via a\n`CouponAppliedListener`), the very next call to `CartService.addSeat()` or\n"
  }
}
```

```json
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_zV78euTWzTDWbKv3SABD75bb",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 15d3ca\nWall time: 0.0522 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2322\nOutput:\n# Pre-FR7 Gap Fixes: Flows 1–6 Audit Report\n\n**Date:** 2026-03-05  \n**Scope:** Gaps in flows 1–6 that would block FR7 (Coupon & Discount Engine) implementation  \n**Status:** All 6 gaps fixed and verified (booking-inventory 80/80 ✅, payments-ticketing 67/67 ✅)\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Background](#background)\n2. [GAP-1 — `BookingConfirmedEvent` missing `bookingId`](#gap-1--bookingconfirmedevent-missing-bookingid)\n3. [GAP-2 — `CartSnapshotReader` exposes only line items, not cart header](#gap-2--cartsnapshotreader-exposes-only-line-items-not-cart-header)\n4. [GAP-3 — `CartService.confirm()` ignores coupon discount in Stripe amount](#gap-3--cartserviceconfirm-ignores-coupon-discount-in-stripe-amount)\n5. [GAP-4 — `CartPricingService.recompute()` overwrites coupon discount](#gap-4--cartpricingservicerecompute-overwrites-coupon-discount)\n6. [GAP-5 — `promotions` module not scaffolded](#gap-5--promotions-module-not-scaffolded)\n7. [GAP-6 — `CouponAppliedEvent` and `CouponValidatedEvent` missing from `shared/`](#gap-6--couponappliedevent-and-couponvalidatedevent-missing-from-shared)\n8. [Test Results After Fixes](#test-results-after-fixes)\n9. [Files Changed Index](#files-changed-index)\n\n---\n\n## Background\n\nBefore implementing FR7 (Promotions / Coupon Engine) a full code audit was performed\nacross flows 1–6.  Six gaps were found that would cause either:\n\n- **Data integrity failures** (wrong Stripe amount charged to users)\n- **Broken cross-module communication** (promotions cannot record redemptions without `bookingId`)\n- **Silent data clobbering** (coupon discount overwritten by group discount on every recompute)\n- **Compilation failures** (promotions module doesn't exist, shared events missing)\n\nAll 6 gaps were fixed in the **correct dependency order** so that no fix breaks a previous one:\n\n```\nGAP-1  →  GAP-6  →  GAP-2  →  GAP-4  →  GAP-3  →  GAP-5\nshared     shared     shared     Cart       CartSvc    scaffold\nevents     events     interface  entity +   total      promotions\n                                 migration  compute    module\n```\n\n---\n\n## GAP-1 — `BookingConfirmedEvent` missing `bookingId`\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL\n\n### Root Cause\n\n`BookingConfirmedEvent` was defined with four fields:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE (broken)\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    String stripePaymentIntentId,\n    Long userId\n) {}\n```\n\n`PaymentService.confirmPayment()` published this event without including `booking.getId()`.\n\n### Why It Blocks FR7\n\nThe `promotions` module must create a `CouponRedemption` row when a booking is confirmed.\n`CouponRedemption` has a `booking_id` foreign key so that:\n\n- The redemption is tied to a specific booking (not just a cart, which is transient)\n- Cancellations can look up the redemption via `bookingId` to trigger refund of the coupon\n- The uniqueness constraint `(coupon_id, booking_id)` prevents double-redemption\n\nWithout `bookingId` in the event, the `BookingConfirmedListener` in `promotions` would\nhave to do an extra JOIN back to `bookings` (cross-module DB coupling — **HARD RULE #1 violation**)\nor simply not be able to create the `CouponRedemption` at all.\n\n### Fix\n\n**`shared/…/event/published/BookingConfirmedEvent.java`**\n```java\n// AFTER (fixed)\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    Long bookingId,          // ← ADDED — needed by promotions.BookingConfirmedListener\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    String stripePaymentIntentId,\n    Long userId\n) {}\n```\n\n**`payments-ticketing/…/service/PaymentService.java`** — publish site updated:\n```java\n// BEFORE\nafterCommitEventPublisher.publish(new BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    booking.getCartId(),\n    seatIds,\n    paymentIntentId,\n    booking.getUserId()\n));\n\n// AFTER\nafterCommitEventPublisher.publish(new BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    booking.getId(),         // ← bookingId added\n    booking.getCartId(),\n    seatIds,\n    paymentIntentId,\n    booking.getUserId()\n));\n```\n\n**`BookingConfirmedListener` in `booking-inventory`** — unaffected (it only reads\n`cartId`, `seatIds`, `stripePaymentIntentId`, `userId` — all still present, order didn't\nchange for those accessors because Java records use named accessor methods not positional).\n\n**Tests** — `PaymentServiceTest` uses `ArgumentCaptor`, not a constructor call, so no\nchanges needed.\n\n---\n\n## GAP-2 — `CartSnapshotReader` exposes only line items, not cart header\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL\n\n### Root Cause\n\nThe `CartSnapshotReader` interface in `shared/` had only one method:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE (incomplete)\npublic interface CartSnapshotReader {\n    List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);\n}\n```\n\n`CartItemSnapshotDto` records per-line-item data (seatId, ticketClassId, unitPrice, quantity)\nbut nothing about the cart itself.\n\n### Why It Blocks FR7\n\n`CouponEligibilityService` (in `promotions`) must validate a coupon against:\n\n| Context field needed | Where it lives           |\n|----------------------|--------------------------|\n| `orgId`              | `Cart.orgId`             |\n| `slotId`             | `Cart.showSlotId`        |\n| `couponCode`         | `Cart.couponCode`        |\n| `expiresAt`          | `Cart.expiresAt`         |\n| `currency`           | `Cart.currency` (via embedded Money) |\n\nWithout this data, the eligibility check would need to:\n1. Call another module's `@Repository` directly — **HARD RULE #1 violation**, or\n2. Make a REST call to an endpoint that didn't exist, or\n3. Duplicate the query logic inside `promotions` — wrong\n\n### Fix\n\n**New shared DTO** — `shared/…/dto/CartSummaryDto.java`:\n```java\npublic record CartSummaryDto(\n    Long    cartId,\n    Long    orgId,\n    Long    slotId,\n    String  couponCode,\n    Instant expiresAt,\n    String  currency\n) {}\n```\n\n**Extended interface** — `CartSnapshotReader.java`:\n```java\npublic interface CartSnapshotReader {\n    List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);\n\n    /**\n     * Returns cart-level header metadata.\n     * Used by promotions.CouponEligibilityService.\n     * Throws ResourceNotFoundException if cart does not exist.\n     */\n    CartSummaryDto getCartSummary(Long cartId);\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation** — `CartSnapshotReaderImpl.java` in `booking-inventory`:\n- Added `CartRepository` dependency via constructor injection\n- New `getCartSummary()` method loads the cart by ID and maps its fields to `CartSummaryDto`\n- Throws `ResourceNotFoundException(404)` if cart not found — consistent error contract\n\n---\n\n## GAP-3 — `CartService.confirm()` ignores coupon discount in Stripe amount\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL (financial bug — user over-charged)\n\n### Root Cause\n\n`CartService.confirm()` computed the Stripe PaymentIntent amount as:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE (financially incorrect)\nCartPricingResult pricing = cartPricingService.recompute(cart, confirmedItems);\nlong totalAmountInSmallestUnit = pricing.total().amount()\n    .multiply(java.math.BigDecimal.valueOf(100)).longValue();\n```\n\n`CartPricingResult.total()` only accounts for the group/bulk discount\n(`subtotal − groupDiscount`).  The coupon discount stored on `cart.couponDiscountAmount`\nwas never read here.\n\n### Impact if Unfixed\n\n1. User applies a ₹500 coupon to a ₹2000 cart\n2. Promotions module writes `couponDiscountAmount = 500.00` to the cart\n3. Cart confirm is triggered — the amount Stripe receives is **₹2000** (net of only the\n   group discount, ignoring the coupon)\n4. User is billed the **full pre-coupon price**\n5. The coupon usage counter increments, but no actual discount was given\n\n### Fix\n\n```java\n// AFTER (fixed)\nCartPricingResult pricing = cartPricingService.recompute(cart, confirmedItems);\n\n// Subtract coupon discount (written by promotions via CouponAppliedEvent) from the\n// post-group-discount total.  Floor at zero to guard against oversized coupons.\njava.math.BigDecimal netTotal = pricing.total().amount()\n    .subtract(cart.getCouponDiscountAmount().amount())\n    .max(java.math.BigDecimal.ZERO);                         // ← safety floor\nlong totalAmountInSmallestUnit = netTotal\n    .multiply(java.math.BigDecimal.valueOf(100))\n    .setScale(0, java.math.RoundingMode.HALF_UP)\n    .longValue();\nString pricingCurrency = pricing.total().currency().toLowerCase();\n```\n\nThe `.max(BigDecimal.ZERO)` guard ensures that even if an edge-case coupon value exceeds\nthe remaining total (e.g., a 100%-off promotional code), the Stripe amount is clamped at\nzero rather than going negative (which would cause a Stripe API error).\n\n**Dependency:** This fix depends on GAP-4 (the `getCouponDiscountAmount()` method must\nexist on `Cart`) — hence GAP-4 was applied first.\n\n---\n\n## GAP-4 — `CartPricingService.recompute()` overwrites coupon discount\n\n### Severity: CRITICAL (data clobbering — discount silently lost)\n\n### Root Cause\n\n`Cart.java` had a **single `discountAmount` field** mapped to a single `discount_amount`\ndatabase column.  `CartPricingService.recompute()` always wrote the group discount into it:\n\n```java\n// BEFORE — overwrites whatever was there\ncart.setDiscountAmount(discount);  // discount = group discount only\n```\n\nIf the promotions module wrote a coupon discount to the cart (e.g., via a\n`CouponAppliedListener`), the very next call to `CartService.addSeat()` or\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 34
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T21:35:47.257Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T21:35:47.257Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_z8eVnJCn2RWO91N5T1I33kGo",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 084a3f\nWall time: 0.0506 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 4471\nOutput:\n# PRODUCT.md — Module Registry & Product Map\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026 — FR5 updated: Stripe replaces Eventbrite Checkout Widget for payments and refunds\n\n**Architecture:** Modular Monolith — Spring Boot 3.5.11, Java 21\n\n**External Integrations:** Eventbrite (event listing sync + organizer webhooks only) | Stripe (payments + refunds) | OpenAI GPT-4o (AI assistant)\n\n⚠ **Important:** This is the single source of truth for module ownership, boundaries, inter-module communication, and the Eventbrite ACL. Every agent and developer MUST read this before touching any module.\n\n---\n\n## 1. The 7 Modules — What Each One Owns\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Module | Owns | Must Not Own |\n|---|---|---|\n| discovery-catalog | Events, Venues, Cities, Eventbrite catalog sync | Bookings, Users, Payments |\n| scheduling | Show slots, time slot config, conflict validation, Eventbrite event sync | Bookings, Users |\n| identity | Users, JWT auth, profiles, preferences | Bookings, Payments, Events |\n| booking-inventory | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Seat Lock State Machine, Pricing | Payments, Reviews, Users |\n| payments-ticketing | Bookings, Payments (Stripe), E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds (Stripe), Wallet | Seats (reads cart only) |\n| promotions | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules, EB discount sync | Bookings, Payments, Users |\n| engagement | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot, RAG pipeline | Bookings (reads attended status only) |\n\n- `admin/` — owns no domain. Orchestrates and aggregates across modules. No `@Entity` classes.\n- `shared/` — owns all external integrations (Eventbrite ACL), JWT, base classes, common DTOs.\n- `app/` — entry point only. `@SpringBootApplication`. Zero business logic.\n\n## 2. Module Dependency Order\n\n```text\nshared/               ← foundational — no dependency on any module\n    ↓\ndiscovery-catalog     ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nscheduling            ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads venue/event via REST)\n    ↓\nidentity              ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nbooking-inventory     ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads seat layout via REST);\n                          scheduling slot/pricing data via SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader;\n                          payment confirmation status via PaymentConfirmationReader\n                          (all three: shared interfaces, implemented in owning module, injected at runtime — no HTTP)\n    ↓\npayments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity;\n                          cart snapshot data via CartSnapshotReader\n                          (shared interface, implemented in booking-inventory, injected at runtime — no HTTP)\n    ↓\npromotions            ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nengagement            ← depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nadmin/                ← thin orchestration — reads from all modules, writes via REST APIs\n    ↓\napp/                  ← entry point only\n```\n\n**Rule:** A module NEVER imports from a module at the same level or below it. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports.\n\n## 3. How Modules Communicate — 3 Rules\n\n### Rule 1 — Never import another module's `@Service` or `@Repository` directly\nIf Module B needs data from Module A, it calls Module A's REST endpoint.\n\n**Approved exception — Shared Reader Interfaces (in-process reads only):**\nFor hot-path, high-frequency reads in a modular monolith, a module may define a read-only interface in `shared/common/service/` and implement it in the owning module. The consuming module injects the `shared/` interface — never the owning module's concrete `@Service`. This avoids HTTP overhead on the critical path while preserving module boundaries.\n\nCurrent implementations:\n- `SlotSummaryReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `SlotSummaryReaderImpl` in `scheduling` → consumed by `booking-inventory` (slot validation path)\n- `SlotPricingReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `SlotPricingReaderImpl` in `scheduling` → consumed by `booking-inventory` (pricing/provisioning path)\n- `CartSnapshotReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `CartSnapshotReaderImpl` in `booking-inventory` → consumed by `payments-ticketing` (payment confirmation hot path — replaces internal HTTP `GET /internal/booking/carts/{cartId}/items`)\n- `PaymentConfirmationReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `PaymentConfirmationReaderImpl` in `payments-ticketing` → consumed by `booking-inventory` `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` (replaces internal HTTP `GET /api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}`)\n\nRules for this pattern:\n- Interface and DTOs MUST live in `shared/common/service/` and `shared/common/dto/` respectively — never in the owning module\n- Implementation is `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` — no writes\n- Consuming module caches results where appropriate (`CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()`) to avoid repeated reads\n- REST endpoints for the same data are preserved for admin/external consumers\n\n### Rule 2 — Use Spring Events for async reactions\nWhen something important happens in Module A, it publishes a Spring `ApplicationEvent`. Other modules react by registering `@EventListener` methods. No direct calls.\n\n- booking-inventory → publishes `CartAssembledEvent`\n- payments-ticketing → listens `CartAssembledListener` → initiates payment\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `PaymentFailedEvent`\n- booking-inventory → listens `PaymentFailedListener` → triggers seat lock rollback\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `BookingConfirmedEvent`\n- engagement → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → unlocks review eligibility\n- identity → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → updates order history\n\n### Rule 3 — All Eventbrite API calls go through `shared/eventbrite/service/` only\nNo module ever calls Eventbrite directly. The ACL facade services are the only entry point.\nInbound Eventbrite webhooks: `EbWebhookController → EbWebhookDispatcher → dispatched as Spring Events → modules listen`.\nModules never consume the raw webhook directly.\n\n## 4. Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n\n### ACL Facade Services\n\n- **EbEventSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create (org-scoped), update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity\n  \n- **EbVenueService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create venues under an org (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); update venues (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`); list org venues (`GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); list events at a venue (`GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`).\n  \n- **EbScheduleService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create recurring event schedules and occurrences; retrieve series info\n  \n- **EbTicketService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n  \n- **EbCapacityService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; list org seat maps; attach seat map to event\n  \n- **EbOrderService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve order details for org-level reporting only. **NOT used in FR5 or FR6 payment flows** — Stripe owns all payment and order management. Retained only for organizer-side admin reporting and orphan detection.\n  \n- **EbAttendeeService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. Used in FR8 (attendance verification for reviews) only. **No longer used in FR5 payment flows** — Stripe and internal DB own all booking records.\n  \n- **EbDiscountSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update/delete discount codes at org level; sync usage with internal promotions. (Discounts scoped to org, applied to events at checkout.)\n  \n- **EbRefundService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **Inactive for FR5/FR6.** All refunds are now processed via `StripeRefundService`. Retained in code for legacy compatibility only. Do NOT call for any new refund flow.\n  \n- **EbWebhookService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **NOT USED in production.** Webhook registration not available via API; only via Dashboard. Mock service simulates webhook delivery for testing.\n\n> **Note:** `EbOrderService` is **not used in FR5 (payment) or FR6 (refund) flows**. Stripe owns all payment, order, and refund processing. `EbOrderService` is retained only for organizer-side admin reporting.\n\n> **Note:** `EbAttendeeService` is used **only in FR8** (attendance verification before review submission). It reads attendee records to confirm the user attended the event.\n\n> **Note:** `EbRefundService` is **inactive**. All refunds are processed via Stripe (`StripeRefundService`). The EB facade is retained in code for legacy compatibility only.\n\n> **Note:** `EbVenueService` supports venue creation (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`) and updates (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`). The Eventbrite API v3 exposes both endpoints. Venue creation in your app always pushes to Eventbrite and stores the returned `eb_venue_id`.\n\n### Stripe ACL Facade Services (Payments & Refunds)\n\nAll Stripe-related calls go through these three facades in `shared/stripe/service/`. No module calls Stripe HTTP directly.\n\n- **StripePaymentService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/payment_intents` — create PaymentIntent; returns `client_secret` for frontend Stripe Element\n  - `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}` — retrieve and verify status (`succeeded`) and `amount_received` before confirming booking\n  - Stores `stripe_payment_intent_id` and `stripe_charge_id` (`latest_charge`) in `payments` table\n\n- **StripeRefundService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/refunds` — create full or partial refund by `payment_intent` ID; supports `reason` field\n  - `GET /v1/refunds/{id}` — retrieve async refund status (`pending`, `succeeded`, `failed`)\n  - Saves `stripe_refund_id` to `refunds` table\n\n- **StripeWebhookHandler** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - Verifies `Stripe-Signature` header via `Webhook.constructEvent(payload, sigHeader, whsecret)`\n  - Routes events as Spring Events: `payment_intent.succeeded` → idempotent booking confirm; `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `payment_intent.canceled` → `PaymentFailedEvent`; `refund.updated` → update refund status; `refund.failed` → alert ops + notify user\n  - Registered via Stripe Dashboard (Test mode) at `POST /api/webhooks/stripe`\n\n> **Stripe Sandbox:** Use `sk_test_...` / `pk_test_...` keys. Test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` for success, `4000 0000 0000 0002` for decline, `4000 0000 0000 7726` for async refund simulation. See `docs/FR5-reworked-v2.md` and `docs/stripe-api-reference.md` for full test card matrix.\n\n### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n\n**Location:** `mock-eventbrite-api/` (Python FastAPI, in-memory)\n\n**Purpose:** Enable local development and integration testing without making real API calls to production Eventbrite. All 10 ACL façades route through this mock service when `spring.profiles.active=mock`.\n\n**Architecture:** Python FastAPI server (async, non-blocking) provides full mock implementations of Eventbrite API v3 endpoints. Data is stored in-memory (no database persistence by default). For integration tests, add SQLite or PostgreSQL backend to the mock service.\n\n**Endpoints Mocked:**\n- `POST/GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` \n- `POST/GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/`\n- `POST/GET /ticket_classes/`\n- `POST/GET /inventory_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /capacity_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /orders/`\n- `POST/GET /attendees/`\n- `POST/GET /discounts/`\n- `POST/GET /webhooks/` (webhook registration)\n- ... and 100+ supporting endpoints\n\n**Configuration:**\n- **Spring Profile:** `mock` (Spring Boot automatically routes all ACL façade calls to mock service)\n- **Environment Variable:** `EVENTBRITE_API_URL=http://localhost:9000/v3/` (when using mock profile)\n- **Startup Command:** \n  ```bash\n  cd mock-eventbrite-api\n  python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9000 --reload\n  ```\n\n**Trade-Offs:**\n| Aspect | Behavior |\n|---|---|\n| **Data Persistence** | In-memory; lost on shutdown. Acceptable for rapid iteration. |\n| **Latency** | Minimal (<5ms); no real network calls. |\n| **API Fidelity** | Matches Eventbrite API v3 schema exactly; test against real contract. |\n| **Concurrency** | Handles parallel requests; respects single-flight locks for testing. |\n| **Webhooks** | Simulates webhook delivery for testing event-driven flows. |\n\n**Usage Pattern:**\n- **Local Dev:** Run mock service; set `spring.profiles.active=mock` in `application-mock.properties`\n- **Integration Tests:** Use Spring `@ActiveProfiles(\"mock\")` to inject mock implementations\n- **Production:** Set `spring.profiles.active=production`; real Eventbrite API calls occur\n\n**See** `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` **for detailed mock service documentation, sample payloads, and configuration options.**\n\n## 5. Eventbrite Integration Principles\n\n**Core Architecture:** Your internal DB is the primary source of truth. **Stripe is the payment and refund backbone** (FR5, FR6). **Eventbrite is a passive event listing sync layer** — it hosts the public event listing, tracks ticket class capacity, and fires organizer-side webhooks (`event.updated`, `event.unpublished`). Every entity is created internally first — then pushed to Eventbrite for listing purposes. The `eventbrite_event_id` returned from each push is stored in your DB and is the bridge for listing sync.\n\n### 5.1 Internal-First, Then Eventbrite\n\nThe pattern for every flow that touches Eventbrite is always:\n\n1. User/Admin performs action in YOUR app\n2. Internal DB is updated / validated first\n3. Eventbrite API is called via `shared/` ACL facade\n4. Eventbrite returns ID or confirmation\n5. Your DB stores the Eventbrite ID (`eventbrite_event_id`, `eventbrite_venue_id`, etc.)\n6. Both systems are now in sync — your DB is source of truth\n7. For payments and refunds: Stripe is called instead of Eventbrite — `stripe_payment_intent_id` and `stripe_charge_id` are stored in your `payments` table\n\n### 5.2 The `eventbrite_event_id` is the Spine of the System\n\nEvery downstream flow depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being present and valid in your DB:\n- FR5 (Payment) — event listing on Eventbrite uses the `event_id`; Stripe drives the actual payment, so EB checkout widget is **not required** for payment to succeed, but EB passive sync still depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being valid\n- FR6 (Cancellation — organizer-initiated) — `event.updated` / `event.unpublished` webhooks from EB identify the event to bulk-cancel via `eventbrite_event_id`\n- FR7 (Discounts) — Eventbrite discount codes are scoped to `event_id`\n- FR8 (Reviews) — Verifying attended booking cross-references Eventbrite attendee list by `event_id`\n- FR10 (Seat Lock) — Availability check hits Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n\nWithout FR2 sync: If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, EB listing sync and capacity tracking break. However, payments (FR5) and refunds (FR6 buyer-initiated) are fully functional through Stripe regardless.\n\n### 5.3 What Eventbrite Cannot Do — Internal Responsibilities\n\n| Gaps | Handled Internally |\n|---|---|\n| User creation/sync | No user write API on Eventbrite. Identity is 100% internal. Users are linked via order email post-purchase. |\n| Conflict validation | No conflict API on Eventbrite. Time slot conflicts and turnaround gaps are enforced in your internal DB. |\n| \"Venue creation — POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ is available via EbVenueService.createVenue(). Admin creates venues in your app; they are pushed to Eventbrite and eb_venue_id is stored. Venue updates via POST /venues/{venue_id}/. The Eventbrite Dashboard can also manage venues independently — VenueReconciliationJob detects and flags drift.\"|\n| Seat locking | No seat lock API on Eventbrite. The entire state machine (Redis + Spring State Machine) is internal. |\n| Cart management | No cart API on Eventbrite. Cart assembly and group discount rules are internal. |\n| Order creation | Orders are created **internally** in your `bookings` table when Stripe payment is confirmed. No order creation via Eventbrite API at any point. |\n| Attendee creation | Attendee records for FR8 attendance verification come from the Eventbrite attendee list (organizer hosted the event). No attendee creation API called by this app. |\n| Payment processing | **Stripe** handles all payments. Backend creates a `PaymentIntent` (`POST /v1/payment_intents`), frontend renders Stripe Payment Element with `client_secret`, user pays, backend verifies via `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}`. Eventbrite has no role in payment. |\n| Single order cancel (buyer) | Buyer cancellations call `POST /v1/refunds` via `StripeRefundService`. Full or partial refund by `payment_intent` ID. No Eventbrite API call for buyer-initiated cancellations. |\n| Refund submission | All refunds use `StripeRefundService.createRefund()` — programmatic, synchronous or async. Refund status tracked via `refund.updated` webhook. Eventbrite has no refund role. |\n| Webhook registration | No webhook API. Webhooks are registered via Eventbrite Dashboard only. This app simulates webhooks for testing/development. |\n| Reviews | No reviews API on Eventbrite. Entire review system is internal. Attendance verification uses attendee read API. |\n| Public event search | `GET /events/search/` is deprecated. Event discovery is limited to your org's events. |\n\n## 6. Functional Requirement Flows — Internal + Eventbrite Sync\n\n### FR1 — City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n**Module:** discovery-catalog\n\n"
  }
}
```
