Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n\n### ACL Facade Services\n\n- **EbEventSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create (org-scoped), update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity\n  \n- **EbVenueService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create venues under an org (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); update venues (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`); list org venues (`GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); list events at a venue (`GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`).\n  \n- **EbScheduleService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create recurring event schedules and occurrences; retrieve series info\n  \n- **EbTicketService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n  \n- **EbCapacityService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; list org seat maps; attach seat map to event\n  \n- **EbOrderService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve order details for org-level reporting only. **NOT used in FR5 or FR6 payment flows** — Stripe owns all payment and order management. Retained only for organizer-side admin reporting and orphan detection.\n  \n- **EbAttendeeService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. Used in FR8 (attendance verification for reviews) only. **No longer used in FR5 payment flows** — Stripe and internal DB own all booking records.\n  \n- **EbDiscountSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update/delete discount codes at org level; sync usage with internal promotions. (Discounts scoped to org, applied to events at checkout.)\n  \n- **EbRefundService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **Inactive for FR5/FR6.** All refunds are now processed via `StripeRefundService`. Retained in code for legacy compatibility only. Do NOT call for any new refund flow.\n  \n- **EbWebhookService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **NOT USED in production.** Webhook registration not available via API; only via Dashboard. Mock service simulates webhook delivery for testing.\n\n> **Note:** `EbOrderService` is **not used in FR5 (payment) or FR6 (refund) flows**. Stripe owns all payment, order, and refund processing. `EbOrderService` is retained only for organizer-side admin reporting.\n\n> **Note:** `EbAttendeeService` is used **only in FR8** (attendance verification before review submission). It reads attendee records to confirm the user attended the event.\n\n> **Note:** `EbRefundService` is **inactive**. All refunds are processed via Stripe (`StripeRefundService`). The EB facade is retained in code for legacy compatibility only.\n\n> **Note:** `EbVenueService` supports venue creation (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`) and updates (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`). The Eventbrite API v3 exposes both endpoints. Venue creation in your app always pushes to Eventbrite and stores the returned `eb_venue_id`.\n\n### Stripe ACL Facade Services (Payments & Refunds)\n\nAll Stripe-related calls go through these three facades in `shared/stripe/service/`. No module calls Stripe HTTP directly.\n\n- **StripePaymentService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/payment_intents` — create PaymentIntent; returns `client_secret` for frontend Stripe Element\n  - `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}` — retrieve and verify status (`succeeded`) and `amount_received` before confirming booking\n  - Stores `stripe_payment_intent_id` and `stripe_charge_id` (`latest_charge`) in `payments` table\n\n- **StripeRefundService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/refunds` — create full or partial refund by `payment_intent` ID; supports `reason` field\n  - `GET /v1/refunds/{id}` — retrieve async refund status (`pending`, `succeeded`, `failed`)\n  - Saves `stripe_refund_id` to `refunds` table\n\n- **StripeWebhookHandler** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - Verifies `Stripe-Signature` header via `Webhook.constructEvent(payload, sigHeader, whsecret)`\n  - Routes events as Spring Events: `payment_intent.succeeded` → idempotent booking confirm; `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `payment_intent.canceled` → `PaymentFailedEvent`; `refund.updated` → update refund status; `refund.failed` → alert ops + notify user\n  - Registered via Stripe Dashboard (Test mode) at `POST /api/webhooks/stripe`\n\n> **Stripe Sandbox:** Use `sk_test_...` / `pk_test_...` keys. Test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` for success, `4000 0000 0000 0002` for decline, `4000 0000 0000 7726` for async refund simulation. See `docs/FR5-reworked-v2.md` and `docs/stripe-api-reference.md` for full test card matrix.\n\n### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n\n**Location:** `mock-eventbrite-api/` (Python FastAPI, in-memory)\n\n**Purpose:** Enable local development and integration testing without making real API calls to production Eventbrite. All 10 ACL façades route through this mock service when `spring.profiles.active=mock`.\n\n**Architecture:** Python FastAPI server (async, non-blocking) provides full mock implementations of Eventbrite API v3 endpoints. Data is stored in-memory (no database persistence by default). For integration tests, add SQLite or PostgreSQL backend to the mock service.\n\n**Endpoints Mocked:**\n- `POST/GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` \n- `POST/GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/`\n- `POST/GET /ticket_classes/`\n- `POST/GET /inventory_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /capacity_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /orders/`\n- `POST/GET /attendees/`\n- `POST/GET /discounts/`\n- `POST/GET /webhooks/` (webhook registration)\n- ... and 100+ supporting endpoints\n\n**Configuration:**\n- **Spring Profile:** `mock` (Spring Boot automatically routes all ACL façade calls to mock service)\n- **Environment Variable:** `EVENTBRITE_API_URL=http://localhost:9000/v3/` (when using mock profile)\n- **Startup Command:** \n  ```bash\n  cd mock-eventbrite-api\n  python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9000 --reload\n  ```\n\n**Trade-Offs:**\n| Aspect | Behavior |\n|---|---|\n| **Data Persistence** | In-memory; lost on shutdown. Acceptable for rapid iteration. |\n| **Latency** | Minimal (<5ms); no real network calls. |\n| **API Fidelity** | Matches Eventbrite API v3 schema exactly; test against real contract. |\n| **Concurrency** | Handles parallel requests; respects single-flight locks for testing. |\n| **Webhooks** | Simulates webhook delivery for testing event-driven flows. |\n\n**Usage Pattern:**\n- **Local Dev:** Run mock service; set `spring.profiles.active=mock` in `application-mock.properties`\n- **Integration Tests:** Use Spring `@ActiveProfiles(\"mock\")` to inject mock implementations\n- **Production:** Set `spring.profiles.active=production`; real Eventbrite API calls occur\n\n**See** `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` **for detailed mock service documentation, sample payloads, and configuration options.**\n\n## 5. Eventbrite Integration Principles\n\n**Core Architecture:** Your internal DB is the primary source of truth. **Stripe is the payment and refund backbone** (FR5, FR6). **Eventbrite is a passive event listing sync layer** — it hosts the public event listing, tracks ticket class capacity, and fires organizer-side webhooks (`event.updated`, `event.unpublished`). Every entity is created internally first — then pushed to Eventbrite for listing purposes. The `eventbrite_event_id` returned from each push is stored in your DB and is the bridge for listing sync.\n\n### 5.1 Internal-First, Then Eventbrite\n\nThe pattern for every flow that touches Eventbrite is always:\n\n1. User/Admin performs action in YOUR app\n2. Internal DB is updated / validated first\n3. Eventbrite API is called via `shared/` ACL facade\n4. Eventbrite returns ID or confirmation\n5. Your DB stores the Eventbrite ID (`eventbrite_event_id`, `eventbrite_venue_id`, etc.)\n6. Both systems are now in sync — your DB is source of truth\n7. For payments and refunds: Stripe is called instead of Eventbrite — `stripe_payment_intent_id` and `stripe_charge_id` are stored in your `payments` table\n\n### 5.2 The `eventbrite_event_id` is the Spine of the System\n\nEvery downstream flow depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being present and valid in your DB:\n- FR5 (Payment) — event listing on Eventbrite uses the `event_id`; Stripe drives the actual payment, so EB checkout widget is **not required** for payment to succeed, but EB passive sync still depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being valid\n- FR6 (Cancellation — organizer-initiated) — `event.updated` / `event.unpublished` webhooks from EB identify the event to bulk-cancel via `eventbrite_event_id`\n- FR7 (Discounts) — Eventbrite discount codes are scoped to `event_id`\n- FR8 (Reviews) — Verifying attended booking cross-references Eventbrite attendee list by `event_id`\n- FR10 (Seat Lock) — Availability check hits Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n\nWithout FR2 sync: If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, EB listing sync and capacity tracking break. However, payments (FR5) and refunds (FR6 buyer-initiated) are fully functional through Stripe regardless.\n\n### 5.3 What Eventbrite Cannot Do — Internal Responsibilities\n\n| Gaps | Handled Internally |\n|---|---|\n| User creation/sync | No user write API on Eventbrite. Identity is 100% internal. Users are linked via order email post-purchase. |\n| Conflict validation | No conflict API on Eventbrite. Time slot conflicts and turnaround gaps are enforced in your internal DB. |\n| \"Venue creation — POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ is available via EbVenueService.createVenue(). Admin creates venues in your app; they are pushed to Eventbrite and eb_venue_id is stored. Venue updates via POST /venues/{venue_id}/. The Eventbrite Dashboard can also manage venues independently — VenueReconciliationJob detects and flags drift.\"|\n| Seat locking | No seat lock API on Eventbrite. The entire state machine (Redis + Spring State Machine) is internal. |\n| Cart management | No cart API on Eventbrite. Cart assembly and group discount rules are internal. |\n| Order creation | Orders are created **internally** in your `bookings` table when Stripe payment is confirmed. No order creation via Eventbrite API at any point. |\n| Attendee creation | Attendee records for FR8 attendance verification come from the Eventbrite attendee list (organizer hosted the event). No attendee creation API called by this app. |\n| Payment processing | **Stripe** handles all payments. Backend creates a `PaymentIntent` (`POST /v1/payment_intents`), frontend renders Stripe Payment Element with `client_secret`, user pays, backend verifies via `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}`. Eventbrite has no role in payment. |\n| Single order cancel (buyer) | Buyer cancellations call `POST /v1/refunds` via `StripeRefundService`. Full or partial refund by `payment_intent` ID. No Eventbrite API call for buyer-initiated cancellations. |\n| Refund submission | All refunds use `StripeRefundService.createRefund()` — programmatic, synchronous or async. Refund status tracked via `refund.updated` webhook. Eventbrite has no refund role. |\n| Webhook registration | No webhook API. Webhooks are registered via Eventbrite Dashboard only. This app simulates webhooks for testing/development. |\n| Reviews | No reviews API on Eventbrite. Entire review system is internal. Attendance verification uses attendee read API. |\n| Public event search | `GET /events/search/` is deprecated. Event discovery is limited to your org's events. |\n\n## 6. Functional Requirement Flows — Internal + Eventbrite Sync\n\n### FR1 — City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n**Module:** discovery-catalog\n\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T21:35:47.264Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_EmXiLe6okii9WNWmJRYqUoov",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 907f93\nWall time: 0.0513 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3259\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```

Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```
---
## Entry 36
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T21:35:47.275Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T21:35:47.275Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_keanCyssIkJCp3LN18ZKZLis",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 551d5f\nWall time: 0.0507 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2254\nOutput:\n# Eventbrite Integration Strategy\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026  \n**Status:** Partially Implemented — See Constraints Section\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nEventbrite serves as the **ticketing and payment backbone** for the multi-city event booking platform. The architecture follows an **internal-first approach**: your database is the source of truth, and Eventbrite is pushed to after internal validation.\n\n**Core Principle:** Eventbrite does not create orders or users. It only provides ticketing infrastructure, payment processing through its checkout widget, and attendee records post-purchase.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Eventbrite ACL Facades (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n### Available Facades\n\n| Facade | Responsibilities | Status |\n|---|---|---|\n| **EbEventSyncService** | Create, update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbVenueService** | Create and update venues on Eventbrite; list venues by org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbScheduleService** | Create recurring event schedules and occurrences | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbTicketService** | Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory tiers; check availability | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbCapacityService** | Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; manage seat maps | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbOrderService** | Retrieve order details post-checkout; list orders by event/org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbAttendeeService** | Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event or org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbDiscountSyncService** | Create, update, delete discount codes on Eventbrite; sync with internal promotions | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbRefundService** | Read refund request status on orders; filter orders by refund status | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbWebhookService** | Register and manage org-scoped webhooks for event/order state changes | ✅ Designed |\n\n**Hard Rule:** No module ever calls Eventbrite directly. All API calls go through these facades in `shared/`.\n\n---\n\n## 2. The Spine: `eventbrite_event_id`\n\nThe `eventbrite_event_id` is the bridge between your internal DB and Eventbrite. Every downstream flow depends on it:\n\n- **FR2 (Scheduling)** — Create show slot → Store `eventbrite_event_id`\n- **FR4 (Seats)** — Validate availability against Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n- **FR5 (Payment)** — Eventbrite Checkout Widget needs valid `event_id` to render\n- **FR6 (Cancellation)** — Refund APIs reference `event_id` + `order_id`\n- **FR7 (Discounts)** — Discount codes scoped to `event_id`\n- **FR8 (Reviews)** — Attendance verification uses `event_id` + attendee email\n\n**Without FR2 sync:** If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, the entire chain breaks.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Integration Flows — Planned vs. Constraints\n\n### FR1: City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser selects city \n  ↓\nApp queries internal venues by city_id\n  ↓\nGET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nMerge internal event_catalog with Eventbrite org events\n  ↓\nUser sees unified event list\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list org venues for cross-reference\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — pull org's published events\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — fetch individual event details for catalog refresh\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ Public event search (`GET /events/search/`) is **deprecated** on Eventbrite. Event discovery is limited to your org's events only. No city-wide public search available.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Limited to org events\n\n---\n\n### FR2: Show Scheduling → Time Slot Configuration → Conflict Validation\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Phase 1: Venue Registration\n```\nAdmin registers venue in your app\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_venue_id in internal DB\n```\n\n#### Phase 2: Show Slot Creation (Core Sync)\n```\nAdmin creates show slot with venue + date + time\n  ↓\nInternal validation (duplicate check, turnaround gaps)\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/events/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nEventbrite returns event_id\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_event_id in show_slots table\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/ [Eventbrite] — create ticket tiers\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/publish/ [Eventbrite] — make event live\n```\n\n#### Phase 3: Recurring Shows\n```\nAdmin creates recurring series (e.g., every Saturday)\n  ↓\nInternal DB generates individual show_slot records\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/schedules/ [Eventbrite] — create recurring occurrences\n```\n\n#### Phase 4: Status Sync\n```\nAdmin updates/deletes slot\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — sync changes\n  OR\nPOST /events/{event_id}/cancel/ [Eventbrite] — cancel event\n```\n\n#### Phase 5: Conflict Validation (Background Job)\n```\nPeriodic job queries internal show_slots\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — verify eventbrite_event_id still exists\n  ↓\nFlags orphaned records for admin review\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — create venue\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list venues for conflict check\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — create event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/` — create ticket tiers\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/publish/` — publish event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/schedules/` — create recurring occurrences\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/` — update event details\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/cancel/` — cancel event\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — verify event exists\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **Turnaround policy enforcement** — Eventbrite has no conflict validation API. Your app must enforce time gaps between shows internally. Conflict validation is 100% internal DB logic.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Turnaround gaps are internal logic only\n\n---\n\n### FR3: User Registration → Profile Setup → Preference Onboarding\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser registers (email + password + name)\n  ↓\nInternal users table created\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite call — no user creation API exists]\n  ↓\nUser fills profile (phone, city, avatar)\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite sync at this stage]\n  ↓\nUser selects preferences (genres, cities, price ranges)\n  ↓\nPreferences stored in users_preferences table\n  ↓\n[Post-purchase: user linked to Eventbrite via order email]\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- No Eventbrite call for this flow we will use the atendee sync that happens in later flows\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No user creation API on Eventbrite.** User identity is 100% internal. The link between your app user and Eventbrite is established post-purchase via the order email. No user profile sync is possible.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Zero Eventbrite integration at registration time\n\n---\n\n### FR4: Seat Selection → Pricing Tier Validation → Cart Assembly\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser picks seats from internal SeatMap\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nValidate: ticket count still > 0?\n  ↓\nApply internal pricing tiers (VIP, Premium, General)\n  ↓\nPOST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/ [Eventbrite] — validate pricing\n  ↓\nSoft lock seat via Redis (TTL = cart session)\n  ↓\nREST call to promotions module — validate coupon eligibility\n  ↓\nCartAssembledEvent published\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/` — remaining ticket count\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — verify tier capacity\n- `POST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/` — validate price against Eventbrite fees\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/ticket_groups/` — check ticket bundles\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No seat lock API on Eventbrite.** The entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal using Redis + Spring State Machine. Eventbrite is consulted only once — for availability count.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Seat locking is 100% internal\n\n---\n\n### FR5: Payment Gateway → Booking Confirmation → E-Ticket Generation\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Step 1: Checkout Widget Embed\n```\nCartAssembledEvent received by payments-ticketing\n  ↓\nFrontend receives event_id\n  ↓\nRender Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK: eb_widgets.js)\n  ↓\n[No direct REST call — JS SDK handles it]\n```\n\n#### Step 2: User Pays via Widget\n```\nUser enters payment information in Eventbrite widget\n  ↓\nEventbrite processes payment (NOT Razorpay for this flow)\n  ↓\nWidget fires onOrderComplete callback\n  ↓\nFrontend sends order_id to backend\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Booking Confirmation\n```\nBackend receives order_id from frontend\n  ↓\nGET /orders/{order_id}/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nRead full order details (costs, buyer info, status)\n  ↓\nBookingConfirmedEvent published\n  ↓\nengagement module unlocks review eligibility\n  ↓\nidentity module updates order history\n```\n\n#### Step 4: E-Ticket Generation\n"
  }
}
```

Conflict validation is 100% internal DB logic.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Turnaround gaps are internal logic only\n\n---\n\n### FR3: User Registration → Profile Setup → Preference Onboarding\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser registers (email + password + name)\n  ↓\nInternal users table created\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite call — no user creation API exists]\n  ↓\nUser fills profile (phone, city, avatar)\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite sync at this stage]\n  ↓\nUser selects preferences (genres, cities, price ranges)\n  ↓\nPreferences stored in users_preferences table\n  ↓\n[Post-purchase: user linked to Eventbrite via order email]\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- No Eventbrite call for this flow we will use the atendee sync that happens in later flows\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No user creation API on Eventbrite.** User identity is 100% internal. The link between your app user and Eventbrite is established post-purchase via the order email. No user profile sync is possible.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Zero Eventbrite integration at registration time\n\n---\n\n### FR4: Seat Selection → Pricing Tier Validation → Cart Assembly\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser picks seats from internal SeatMap\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nValidate: ticket count still > 0?\n  ↓\nApply internal pricing tiers (VIP, Premium, General)\n  ↓\nPOST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/ [Eventbrite] — validate pricing\n  ↓\nSoft lock seat via Redis (TTL = cart session)\n  ↓\nREST call to promotions module — validate coupon eligibility\n  ↓\nCartAssembledEvent published\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/` — remaining ticket count\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — verify tier capacity\n- `POST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/` — validate price against Eventbrite fees\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/ticket_groups/` — check ticket bundles\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No seat lock API on Eventbrite.** The entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal using Redis + Spring State Machine. Eventbrite is consulted only once — for availability count.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Seat locking is 100% internal\n\n---\n\n### FR5: Payment Gateway → Booking Confirmation → E-Ticket Generation\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Step 1: Checkout Widget Embed\n```\nCartAssembledEvent received by payments-ticketing\n  ↓\nFrontend receives event_id\n  ↓\nRender Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK: eb_widgets.js)\n  ↓\n[No direct REST call — JS SDK handles it]\n```\n\n#### Step 2: User Pays via Widget\n```\nUser enters payment information in Eventbrite widget\n  ↓\nEventbrite processes payment (NOT Razorpay for this flow)\n  ↓\nWidget fires onOrderComplete callback\n  ↓\nFrontend sends order_id to backend\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Booking Confirmation\n```\nBackend receives order_id from frontend\n  ↓\nGET /orders/{order_id}/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nRead full order details (costs, buyer info, status)\n  ↓\nBookingConfirmedEvent published\n  ↓\nengagement module unlocks review eligibility\n  ↓\nidentity module updates order history\n```\n\n#### Step 4: E-Ticket Generation\n"
  }
}
```
---

CouponResponse.java\n│           ├── DiscountBreakdownResponse.java\n│           └── CouponUsageStatsResponse.java\n├── domain/\n│   ├── Promotion.java\n│   ├── Coupon.java\n│   ├── CouponRedemption.java\n│   ├── CouponUsageReservation.java\n│   ├── OrphanEbDiscount.java\n│   └── DiscountReconciliationLog.java\n├── service/\n│   ├── PromotionService.java                ← CRUD + lifecycle, deactivation cascade\n│   ├── CouponEligibilityService.java        ← validation pipeline, soft-hold, idempotency\n│   ├── CouponRedemptionService.java         ← record/void redemptions, usage counters\n│   └── DiscountSyncOrchestrator.java        ← create/delete+recreate/guarded-delete via EbDiscountSyncService\n├── scheduler/\n│   ├── DiscountReconciliationJob.java       ← inbound EB sync: list, diff, flag orphans/drift\n│   └── CouponExpiryJob.java                ← deactivate expired coupons + guarded EB delete + release reservations\n├── repository/\n│   ├── PromotionRepository.java\n│   ├── CouponRepository.java\n│   ├── CouponRedemptionRepository.java\n│   ├── CouponUsageReservationRepository.java\n│   ├── OrphanEbDiscountRepository.java\n│   └── DiscountReconciliationLogRepository.java\n├── mapper/\n│   ├── PromotionMapper.java\n│   ├── CouponMapper.java\n│   └── EbDiscountSyncPayloadMapper.java     ← Coupon + Promotion → EbDiscountSyncPayload value object\n├── event/\n│   └── listener/\n│       ├── BookingConfirmedListener.java    ← listens BookingConfirmedEvent → record redemption\n│       ├── BookingCancelledListener.java    ← listens BookingCancelledEvent → void redemption\n│       └── PaymentFailedListener.java       ← listens PaymentFailedEvent → release reservation\n└── exception/\n    ├── CouponNotFoundException.java\n    ├── CouponExpiredException.java\n    ├── CouponNotYetValidException.java\n    ├── CouponInactiveException.java\n    ├── CouponOrgMismatchException.java\n    ├── CouponEventMismatchException.java\n    ├── CouponUsageLimitReachedException.java\n    ├── CouponPerUserCapReachedException.java\n    ├── CouponCodeConflictException.java\n    └── EbDiscountSyncException.java\n```\n\n---\n\n## Stage 4 — DB Schema\n\n### Pre-FR7 Migration Already Applied\n\n> **V36** (`V36__split_cart_discount_amounts.sql`) was applied as a pre-FR7 gap fix (GAP-4).\n> It renames `carts.discount_amount` → `carts.group_discount_amount` and adds `carts.coupon_discount_amount`.\n> This migration already exists at `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V36__split_cart_discount_amounts.sql`.\n\n### FR7 Flyway Migration: `V37__create_promotions_tables.sql`\n\n> Migration numbering: V35 (FR6 refund audit) → **V36 (pre-FR7 cart discount split, already applied)** → **V37 (FR7 promotions tables — use this number)**\n\n#### Table: `promotions`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE promotions (\n    id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n    org_id            VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    name              VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    discount_type     VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL,           -- PERCENT_OFF | AMOUNT_OFF\n    discount_value    NUMERIC(12,4) NOT NULL,\n    scope             VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL,           -- ORG_WIDE | EVENT_SCOPED\n    eb_event_id       VARCHAR(255)  NULL,              -- null for ORG_WIDE\n    max_usage_limit   INTEGER       NULL,              -- null = unlimited\n    per_user_cap      INTEGER       NULL,              -- null = unlimited\n    valid_from        TIMESTAMPTZ   NOT NULL,\n    valid_until       TIMESTAMPTZ   NOT NULL,\n    status            VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',\n    created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_promotions_org_id    ON promotions(org_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_promotions_status    ON promotions(status);\nCREATE INDEX idx_promotions_eb_event  ON promotions(eb_event_id) WHERE eb_event_id IS NOT NULL;\n```\n\n#### Table: `coupons`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE coupons (\n    id                           UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n    promotion_id                 UUID         NOT NULL REFERENCES promotions(id),\n    org_id                       VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    code                         VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,\n    status                       VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',  -- ACTIVE | INACTIVE | EXHAUSTED\n    redemption_count             INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    eb_discount_id               VARCHAR(255) NULL,\n    eb_sync_status               VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'NOT_SYNCED',\n    eb_quantity_sold_at_last_sync INTEGER     NULL,\n    last_eb_sync_at              TIMESTAMPTZ  NULL,\n    created_at                   TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    updated_at                   TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()\n);\n\n-- No two active coupons with same code in the same org\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_coupons_code_org\n    ON coupons(code, org_id)\n    WHERE status != 'INACTIVE';\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupons_promotion_id   ON coupons(promotion_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupons_eb_discount_id ON coupons(eb_discount_id) WHERE eb_discount_id IS NOT NULL;\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupons_eb_sync_status ON coupons(eb_sync_status);\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupons_status         ON coupons(status);\n```\n\n#### Table: `coupon_redemptions`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE coupon_redemptions (\n    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n    coupon_id       UUID         NOT NULL REFERENCES coupons(id),\n    user_id         UUID         NOT NULL,          -- ID-only ref to identity.users\n    booking_id      UUID         NOT NULL,          -- ID-only ref to payments-ticketing.bookings\n    cart_id         UUID         NOT NULL,          -- ID-only ref to booking-inventory.carts\n    discount_amount NUMERIC(12,4) NOT NULL,\n    currency        VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL,\n    redeemed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    voided          BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,\n    voided_at       TIMESTAMPTZ  NULL\n);\n\n-- At most one non-voided redemption per (coupon, booking)\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_coupon_redemptions_active\n    ON coupon_redemptions(coupon_id, booking_id)\n    WHERE voided = FALSE;\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupon_redemptions_coupon_id  ON coupon_redemptions(coupon_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupon_redemptions_user_id    ON coupon_redemptions(user_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupon_redemptions_booking_id ON coupon_redemptions(booking_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupon_redemptions_cart_id    ON coupon_redemptions(cart_id);\n```\n\n#### Table: `coupon_usage_reservations`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE coupon_usage_reservations (\n    id          UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n    coupon_id   UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES coupons(id),\n    cart_id     UUID        NOT NULL,\n    user_id     UUID        NOT NULL,\n    reserved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    expires_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n    released    BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE\n);\n\n-- At most one active reservation per (coupon, cart)\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_coupon_reservations_active\n    ON coupon_usage_reservations(coupon_id, cart_id)\n    WHERE released = FALSE;\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupon_reservations_coupon_id  ON coupon_usage_reservations(coupon_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupon_reservations_cart_id    ON coupon_usage_reservations(cart_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_coupon_reservations_expires_at ON coupon_usage_reservations(expires_at)\n    WHERE released = FALSE;\n```\n\n#### Table: `orphan_eb_discounts`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE orphan_eb_discounts (\n    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n    eb_discount_id  VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    org_id          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    code            VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,\n    detected_at     TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    reviewed        BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,\n    notes           TEXT         NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_orphan_eb_discounts_org_id ON orphan_eb_discounts(org_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_orphan_eb_discounts_reviewed ON orphan_eb_discounts(reviewed) WHERE reviewed = FALSE;\n```\n\n#### Table: `discount_reconciliation_logs`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE discount_reconciliation_logs (\n    id                        UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n    org_id                    VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\n    run_at                    TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    discounts_checked         INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    drifts_found              INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    orphans_found             INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    externally_deleted_found  INTEGER      NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    actions_taken_summary     TEXT         NULL,\n    error_summary             TEXT         NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_reconciliation_logs_org_id ON discount_reconciliation_logs(org_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_reconciliation_logs_run_at ON discount_reconciliation_logs(run_at);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Stage 5 — API\n\n### Admin — Promotion Management (`ROLE_ADMIN` required on all)\n\n#### `POST /api/v1/promotions`\nCreate a new promotion.\n\n- **Request body:** `PromotionCreateRequest { name, discountType, discountValue, scope, ebEventId?, validFrom, validUntil, maxUsageLimit?, perUserCap? }`\n- **Response:** `201 Created` with `PromotionResponse { id, name, discountType, discountValue, scope, ebEventId, validFrom, validUntil, maxUsageLimit, perUserCap, status, createdAt }`\n- **Errors:** `400` validation failure | `403` not admin\n\n#### `GET /api/v1/promotions`\nList all promotions for the caller's org (paginated).\n\n- **Query params:** `page`, `size`, `status` (filter)\n- **Response:** `200 OK` paginated list of `PromotionResponse`\n\n#### `GET /api/v1/promotions/{promotionId}`\nGet single promotion detail.\n\n- **Response:** `200 OK` with `PromotionResponse`\n- **Errors:** `404 PROMOTION_NOT_FOUND`\n\n#### `PATCH /api/v1/promotions/{promotionId}`\nUpdate promotion validity and usage limits. Triggers guarded EB delete + recreate for all linked synced coupons.\n\n- **Request body:** `PromotionUpdateRequest { validFrom?, validUntil?, maxUsageLimit?, perUserCap? }`\n- **Response:** `200 OK` with updated `PromotionResponse`\n- **Errors:** `404` | `409 PROMOTION_CANNOT_BE_MODIFIED` if any linked coupon has `CANNOT_RESYNC` — response includes `ebSyncStatus` warning field for each affected coupon\n\n#### `DELETE /api/v1/promotions/{promotionId}`\nDeactivate promotion and all linked coupons. Triggers guarded EB delete for all synced coupons.\n\n- **Response:** `204 No Content`\n- **Errors:** `404` | `409 PROMOTION_HAS_ACTIVE_REDEMPTIONS` if any non-voided redemptions exist\n\n---\n\n### Admin — Coupon Management (`ROLE_ADMIN` required on all)\n\n#### `POST /api/v1/promotions/{promotionId}/coupons`\nCreate a coupon code under a promotion. Triggers async EB sync.\n\n- **Request body:** `CouponCreateRequest { code }`\n- **Response:** `201 Created` with `CouponResponse { id, code, promotionId, status, redemptionCount, ebSyncStatus, ebDiscountId, createdAt }`\n- **Errors:** `404 PROMOTION_NOT_FOUND` | `409 COUPON_CODE_CONFLICT`\n\n#### `GET /api/v1/promotions/{promotionId}/coupons`\nList all coupons for a promotion.\n\n- **Response:** `200 OK` paginated list of `CouponResponse`\n\n#### `GET /api/v1/promotions/coupons/{couponCode}/usage`\nGet redemption count and reservation stats for a coupon.\n\n- **Response:** `200 OK` with `CouponUsageStatsResponse { couponCode, status, redemptionCount, activeReservations, voidedRedemptions, ebSyncStatus, lastEbSyncAt, ebQuantitySoldAtLastSync }`\n- **Errors:** `404 COUPON_NOT_FOUND`\n\n#### `DELETE /api/v1/promotions/coupons/{couponCode}`\nDeactivate coupon. Triggers guarded EB delete.\n\n- **Response:** `204 No Content`\n- **Errors:** `404` | `409 COUPON_HAS_ACTIVE_REDEMPTIONS` if non-voided redemptions exist\n"
  }
}
```
